races.name: William Hill Handicap Hurdle (Premier Handicap) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Joyeuse
runners.spotlight: Useful mare whose handicap form at about 2m features a prestigious win last term and respectable third, with cheekpieces fitted, at the latest Cheltenham Festival (soft); ran well behind a very notable rival in one of her 2m4f attempts; likely player in the retained headgear and back up in trip.
runners.comment: Useful mare who ran respectably at the latest Cheltenham Festival; likely player

runners.name: Trustintimes
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 5yo who has form figures of 11112, all at about 2m, since racing on good ground; ran well in a Kempton handicap at Christmas when last seen; improving but goes into unknown territory trip-wise and his chance also perhaps partly depends on the weather.
runners.comment: Improving but goes into unknown territory trip-wise; looks suited by good ground
runners.owner: The Time On Our Hands Boys Syndicate

runners.name: Act Of Authority
runners.spotlight: Finished fifth with cheekpieces applied at the Cheltenham Festival (2m4f, soft) for another sound effort in the Martin Pipe; broadly consistent; likely to give his running in the retained headgear.
runners.comment: Posted another sound effort in the Martin Pipe; broadly consistent

runners.name: Ike Sport
runners.spotlight: Ran respectably in this race 12 months ago (fifth off 3lb higher) and Cheltenham Festival handicap (3m) latest start; otherwise an exposed 8yo who is on a two-year losing spell; unlikely to come out on top.
runners.comment: Ran respectably in this race 12 months ago but otherwise an exposed 8yo

runners.name: Fingle Bridge
runners.spotlight: Has failed to maintain an unbeaten hurdles record since returned this sphere, sweating and well beaten in the Imperial Cup (2m) last time; penultimate effort (2m5f) wasn't bad but this is a stiffer task.
runners.comment: Well beaten last time; penultimate effort wasn't bad but this is a stiffer task

runners.name: Ballykinlar
runners.spotlight: Consistent results in Ireland this term featured three summer wins; not disgraced with midfield finish at the Cheltenham Festival (2m4f, soft) on debut for new stable but needs to improve on that bare form.
runners.comment: Midfield at Cheltenham Festival on debut for new yard; needs to improve

runners.name: Emidio Pepe
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdles winner in France, prevailing narrowly in a Pau handicap (2m1f, very soft) last time; has joined the Skelton team and may be capable of significant further progress; intriguing contender, especially if the market speaks favourably.
runners.comment: Three hurdles wins in France; may improve further; interesting on British debut

runners.name: Indeevar Bleu
runners.spotlight: Absent and had further wind surgery since his ready win over C&D (good to soft) on Boxing Day when proving well suited by the step up from 2m and taking record to 4-7; progressive and remains particularly unexposed at this trip; looks an ideal type for this assignment.
runners.comment: Progressive sort who won readily over C&D (upped from 2m) on Boxing Day; highly respected

runners.name: Top Jimmy
runners.spotlight: Novice hurdler who was let down by his jumping and eventually fell in the Imperial Cup when dropped back to 2m at Sandown most recently; has a solid record otherwise and some strong 2m4f form behind very notable rivals; may resume improvement, provided errors are avoided.
runners.comment: Let down by his jumping last time; has a solid record otherwise; not written off

runners.name: Sir Galahad
runners.spotlight: Solid novice hurdler at 2m from last spring to autumn, completing a four-timer (on good ground) prior to holding his form, running-on sixth in Grade 2 at Cheltenham the final occasion; attractively weighed and has possibilities with this new trip worth exploring.
runners.comment: Attractively weighted and has possibilities with this new trip worth exploring

runners.name: Captain Teague
runners.spotlight: Has perhaps needed his two runs since a lengthy layoff, sixth to Fiercely Proud at Ascot latest; may be ready to hit top form and accordingly looks interesting off current mark when considering his back class (Grade 1 novice winner over 2m4f in December 2023).
runners.comment: May be ready to hit top form; interesting off current mark given his back class

runners.name: Swindon Village
runners.spotlight: Weakened late on (shaped better than bare result) in the 3m Grade 1 novice hurdle at the Cheltenham Festival; won with plenty to spare at Newbury (soft) the time before, taking 2m4f record under rules to 311, and may have more to offer over this distance; interesting handicap debutant.
runners.comment: Record of 311 in 2m4f contests under rules; may have more to offer at this trip

runners.name: Rambo T
runners.spotlight: Landed a notable handicap at Chepstow (2m3f, good; off just 1lb lower) in the autumn; not in anything like the same form since, always behind at the Cheltenham Festival (2m5f) most recently; other preferred.
runners.comment: Has lost his form since winning a notable autumn prize; others preferred

runners.name: Jet To Vegas
runners.spotlight: Returned to hurdles (from fences) with a below-par effort last time in the 2m Morebattle at Kelso, where he had won a 2m2f Grade 2 novice event 12 months earlier; bit to prove in another warm handicap.
runners.comment: Below par in the Morebattle; bit to prove in another warm handicap

runners.name: Bowmore
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly at about 2m and isn't crying out for this return to further; furthermore, he suffered a heavy defeat at the Cheltenham Festival most recently and this is another difficult task.
runners.comment: Campaigned mostly at about 2m; not crying out for this return to further

runners.name: Wellington Arch
runners.spotlight: Justified favouritism in 2m4f event at Uttoxeter (soft) on Midlands National day, going one better than 12 months earlier and setting himself up perfectly for an attempt to win this prize for the second year running; current mark reflects his advancement; again a big player.
runners.comment: Successful in this race in 2025 and Uttoxeter event last time; again a big player

runners.name: Favour And Fortune
runners.spotlight: Acts on any going; successful in major handicap at Ayr two years ago; his two attempts at Aintree were good efforts in the 2023 Formby (second to highly notable rival) and this race last year (finished third); each-way claims back here with Kempton reappearance under his belt.
runners.comment: Placed in this race last year; each-way claims with Kempton reappearance under his belt

runners.name: Fiercely Proud
runners.spotlight: Has gained both handicap wins in useful contests at Ascot (1m7f/2m3f); something to prove back at Aintree and finished behind some of today's rivals at the Cheltenham Festival (2m4f, soft) last time.
runners.comment: Has gained both handicap wins at Ascot; something to prove back at Aintree

runners.name: Hot Fuss
runners.spotlight: Has performed well in several notable handicaps, including a strong success at Windsor (2m; beat the subsequent County winner) in January and good second at the Cheltenham Festival (upped to 2m4f, soft) last time; solid contender who looks set for another big run.
runners.comment: Good second at the Cheltenham Festival upped to 2m4f; set for another big run

runners.name: Fiveonefive
runners.spotlight: Irish challenger who has done his winning at about 2m, mostly at Cartmel where he remains unbeaten; far from consistent and can be opposed in this highly competitive scenario back up in distance.
runners.comment: Has done his winning at about 2m; far from consistent; opposed

runners.name: Faivoir
runners.spotlight: Has lots of form in major handicaps, generally at about 2m, but losing spell goes back three years and he looks unlikely to land this hot contest at the age of 11; remote third at Newbury most recently.
runners.comment: Has lots of good form but losing spell goes back three years

runners.name: Jazzy Matty
runners.spotlight: Better over fences; stays 2m5f; had wind surgery prior to the latest Cheltenham Festival (2m, good) where he very nearly recorded a second success in the Grand Annual; not guaranteed to transfer the form back to hurdles but he is attractively treated off a much lower mark in this sphere.
runners.comment: Nearly recorded a second win in Cheltenham Festival chase; has a lower hurdles mark

races.name: William Hill Mildmay Novices' Chase (Grade 1) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Gold Dancer
runners.spotlight: Experienced novice who had already won three chases by October; progress then stalled until he ran a screamer off top weight (ridden by 7lb claimer) when second in the Jack Richards at the Cheltenham Festival (about 2m4f, good; Regent's Stroll third, Jordans Cross eighth) last month; didn't stay 3m on sole attempt but that came on heavy ground and the experiment looks worth another go; player.
runners.comment: Career best when second at Cheltenham Festival latest; this trip looks worth another go

runners.name: Salver
runners.spotlight: Can take a while to warm up but he impressed when slamming Doyen Quest in December Grade 2 at Sandown (good to soft) and again dispatched that rival at Windsor (also about 3m, soft; first-time cheekpieces) the following month; 25-1 but ran a scorcher, despite little going right, when flying from the rear to grab third in the Grade 1 Brown Advisory (3m1f) at Cheltenham last month; there's potential for a good gallop here, which will suit, and he holds leading claims.
runners.comment: Huge effort when third in Grade 1 at Cheltenham last month; leading candidate

runners.name: Regents Stroll
runners.spotlight: Nephew of Denman; has been a constant work in progress and pretty much lost the plot without a hood when third to Wendigo at Newbury (good to soft) in November; won a match on soft the following month but couldn't contain Miami Magic when second at Cheltenham in January; highly creditable third in the Jack Richards at Cheltenham (also about 2m4f, good; Golden Dancer second, Jordans Cross eighth) last month, however, and now goes up in trip with something deeper possibly lurking inside him.
runners.comment: Mercurial sort but fine effort at Cheltenham last time; up in trip and may do better yet

runners.name: Wendigo
runners.spotlight: Just nabbed on his chase debut (good) in October but stayed on strongly to pick up Newbury Grade 2 (also 2m4f, good to soft; Regent's Stroll third, Crest Of Fortune fourth) the following month; outpaced throughout but never stopped trying when third in Kempton Grade 1 (3m, good; Salver fourth, Crest Of Fortune behind) over Christmas and that was also the case until he fell at the last when 6l down in the Brown Advisory (3m1f, good to soft; Salver third) last month; solid claims.
runners.comment: Likeable; fell at the last when 6l down at Cheltenham latest; surely on the scene

runners.name: Doyen Quest
runners.spotlight: Tends to lead; scored at 1-50 on his chase debut at Warwick in May and impressed when winning by 10l at Exeter (3m, also good) in November; put in his place by Salver on last two starts (good to soft/soft) but comes here fresh having skipped Cheltenham and any drying of the ground would be in his favour; could get them all at it.
runners.comment: Put in his place by Salver on last two starts but arrives fresh; could go well at a price
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late N W Lake

runners.name: Miami Magic
runners.spotlight: Prominent-racer whose flop over hurdles at this meeting last year can be forgiven; scored on his third start over fences when beating Regent's Stroll by 4l at Cheltenham (about 2m4f, good) in January; jumping left plenty to be desired when third in Sandown Grade 1 later that month, however, and the race he won at Newbury (about 3m, good to soft) last time fell apart; he wasn't always fluent that day either but a clear round puts him on the premises.
runners.comment: Smart novice; beat Regent's Stroll in January; on the premises with clean round of jumping

runners.name: Jordans Cross
runners.spotlight: Progressive 6yo who scored on his chase debut here (2m, good) in October; improved at about 2m4f subsequently, looking unlucky when falling at Cheltenham in November before winning his next two, including a fairly valuable event back there in January (soft); disappointing he could not get involved in the Jack Richards at the Cheltenham Festival (Gold Dancer second, Regent's Stroll third) last month but he has often shaped as if this step up in trip will suit; no surprise if he bounces back.
runners.comment: Disappointed last time but previously progressive; new trip has often looked worth a crack

runners.name: Crest Of Fortune
runners.spotlight: Third in the Grade 1 Sefton at this meeting last year but that wasn't the strongest renewal; promising second of 14 on chase debut at Cheltenham (2m4f, good) in October hasn't been built on and he bled when tailed off in Kempton Grade 1 over Christmas; didn't impress with his jumping in February when last seen and needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Hasn't built on chase debut and had various issues on his last two starts; needs a revival

races.name: ThatPrizeGuy Top Novices' Hurdle (Grade 1) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Sober Glory
runners.spotlight: Seems versatile as regards ground; highly progressive sort whose overall record is 7-9; has looked best over hurdles when making the running and impressively defied a big penalty at Newbury for his most recent success; good second in the Supreme at Cheltenham, despite a final-flight error and getting involved in some bumping on the run-in (bare form can be marked up); the pick on ratings.
runners.comment: Highly progressive; good second in the Supreme and that bare form can be marked up

runners.name: Baron Noir
runners.spotlight: Solid record (1312114) in bumpers and over hurdles for current yard, the most recent win at Kempton (2m, good; acts on soft); ties in with Sober Glory on two pieces of form, latest in the Supreme at Cheltenham where he fared best of the runners who raced towards the rear; warrants respect as he's progressive and looks an Aintree type.
runners.comment: Ran well in the Supreme; progressive and looks an Aintree type; warrants respect
runners.owner: Noel Fehily Racing Syndicate- Baron Noir

runners.name: Sinnatra
runners.spotlight: Gained most recent win over 2m3f (straightforward task) but he's not short of speed and his handicap effort at the Cheltenham Festival (2m1f, soft; ran respectably) suggests this sharper scenario is likely to suit; the type who should still have more to offer; possibilities, provided he takes to first-time hood.
runners.comment: Cheltenham Festival effort suggests this sharper scenario will suit; possibilities

runners.name: La Conquiere
runners.spotlight: Has been campaigned exclusively against her own sex; form dipped at the Cheltenham Festival but may well rebound, with her record solid and progressive otherwise; close second in Grade 2 races at this meeting last year (bumper) and Ascot in January; admittedly faces a stiffer task in this first attempt against males.
runners.comment: Form dipped last time but progressive mare otherwise; first run against males

runners.name: Storming George
runners.spotlight: Resumed his progress with a clearcut success in novice handicap at Newbury (2m3f, good to soft) last time; has failed to shine in two Graded attempts over hurdles but may prove a brighter spark back at Aintree, having performed well (fifth of 15) in Grade 2 bumper at this meeting 12 months ago; has won over 2m.
runners.comment: Resumed his progress last time; ran well in bumper at this meeting last year

runners.name: Starting Fifteen
runners.spotlight: Sometimes free-going and has worn a hood since switched to hurdles, opening his account in Limerick event at Christmas; only seventh when 25-1 in Leopardstown Grade 1 at the Dublin Racing Festival (2m, heavy) but gives the impression he may fare better at this level when the ground is less testing.
runners.comment: Sometimes free-going; this may prove more suitable than latest assignment

races.name: JCB Melling Chase (Grade 1) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Grey Dawning
runners.spotlight: Two Grade 1 wins, namely the 2m4f novice chase at the 2024 Cheltenham Festival (soft) and this season's Betfair Chase at Haydock (3m1f, good to soft); ran well for a long way before fading into fourth in last month's Gold Cup at Cheltenham (3m2f, good to soft); this drop back in trip could well be a positive move; effective here and respected in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Ran well for long way in last month's Gold Cup; the drop back in trip could be a good move

runners.name: Heart Wood
runners.spotlight: 8yo who made his Grade 1 breakthrough in the Ryanair at Cheltenham (2m4f, good) last month; that 10l victory was a career-best performance and he will be tough to beat if reproducing the same scintillating form today; ran well in the Mildmay at this meeting in 2024 on sole run here.
runners.comment: 10l win in the Ryanair last month; tough to beat if reproducing same scintillating form

runners.name: LEau du Sud
runners.spotlight: Third of five in Grade 1 Maghull Novices' Chase here (2m, good) 12 months ago and reappeared with a win in the Grade 2 Shloer at Cheltenham (2m, soft) in November; ran well when 12l third in the Champion Chase at Cheltenham (2m, good to soft) last month but doesn't appear to be crying out for this step up in trip, and Harry Skelton rides Grey Dawning.
runners.comment: Pretty solid performer in top 2m chases but doesn't appear to be crying out for this trip
runners.owner: Mason Hogarth Ferguson Hales The Late J Hales

runners.name: JPR One
runners.spotlight: Proved his stamina when readily defying top weight in handicap at Musselburgh (2m4f, good to soft) in January, and he was disputing the lead entering the straight in the Ryanair at Cheltenham (2m4f, good) last month only to fade up the hill; beaten just over 16l by the winner Heart Wood but today's less demanding track should be a big plus; not ruled out each-way.
runners.comment: Bang there for a long way in the Ryanair; this less demanding track is a big plus

runners.name: Saint Segal
runners.spotlight: Progressive front-runner who has won two handicaps this term (2m3f/2m1f, good to firm/soft) and came second in the Grade 2 Game Spirit at Newbury (2m, heavy) in February; a bad mistake at the first fence all but ended his race in the Champion Chase at Cheltenham last month; sent off 50-1 at the Festival and his form has to go to a new level if he's to pose a threat today.
runners.comment: Progressive this season, with two handicap wins, but form needs to go to another new level

runners.name: Solness
runners.spotlight: Tailed off at Leopardstown in February when the soft ground was against him and a visor replaced his usual cheekpieces, which return today; prior to that he posted a third Grade 1 win at Leopardstown (2m1f, yielding) in December; there's a question mark regarding the step back up in trip for this front-runner but his trainer won this twice in a row with Fakir d'Oudairies in 2021 and 2022, and this 8yo arrives fresh after just two starts this winter; chance.
runners.comment: Strong form when winning Grade 1 at Leopardstown in December; chance if seeing it out

runners.name: Gidleigh Park
runners.spotlight: Made mistakes and pulled up in the Clarence House at Ascot in January when last seen; however, prior to that he reappeared with a good second to subsequent Gold Cup runner-up Jango Baie in the Grade 2 1965 Chase at Ascot (2m5f, good) and last season's best performance came when runner-up in the Grade 1 Manifesto Novices' Chase over C&D (good to soft) in April; arrives fresh, whereas most of these ran at the Cheltenham Festival; interesting.
runners.comment: Pulled up in January but arrives fresh and last season's best performance came over C&D

races.name: Randox Topham Handicap Chase (Premier Handicap) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Will The Wise
runners.spotlight: Progressive over fences this season and took another step forward in a first-time tongue-tie (retained) when best of the rest behind easy winner Madara in the 23-runner Plate at last month's Cheltenham Festival (2m4f, good to soft); this lightly raced 7yo is open to further improvement; chance.
runners.comment: Best of the rest behind Madara in the Plate at Cheltenham and open to further improvement

runners.name: Madara
runners.spotlight: 3-1 favourite when easily winning the 23-runner Plate at Cheltenham (2m4f, good to soft) last month in reapplied cheekpieces (retained); hit with a 10lb rise but that may not stop this 7yo, who could still have more to offer.
runners.comment: Easily won the Plate at the Cheltenham Festival last month and 10lb rise may not stop him

runners.name: Boombawn
runners.spotlight: First run over the National fences; inconsistent this season and no match for stablemate Madara (who remains Harry Skelton's mount) when ninth of 23 in the Plate at Cheltenham last month; however, Aintree brought out the best in him last year and he's well handicapped on that form; one to consider.
runners.comment: Only ninth in the Plate at Cheltenham but likes Aintree and he's on a dangerous mark

runners.name: Viroflay
runners.spotlight: Useful hunter chaser last season and he's transferred that form to handicaps, winning at Kempton (3m, good) in December and runner-up there (2m4f, good to soft) in February; he may be better going right-handed but otherwise this front-runner could prove well suited by today's test.
runners.comment: Front-runner who has done well in h'caps this season; could prove well suited by this test

runners.name: Excello
runners.spotlight: 4lb out of the handicap today and his losing sequence is up to 12 races; however, he ran pretty well when outstayed in the Kim Muir at Cheltenham (3m2f, good) last month and is racing off just 2lb higher than when third of 17 over C&D (good to soft) in the Grand Sefton in November; yard has won this five times and he holds each-way possibilities back down in trip.
runners.comment: Third of 17 in the Grand Sefton over C&D in November and he holds each-way possibilities

runners.name: Booster Bob
runners.spotlight: Held by Madara and Will The Wise but running well when falling at final fence in the Plate at Cheltenham (2m4f, good to soft) last month; two wins during a progressive 2025 (2m4f/2m6f, soft/good to soft); each-way shout.
runners.comment: 2 wins last year and running well when falling at final fence in the Plate; each-way shout

runners.name: Matata
runners.spotlight: Better than ever when easily winning at Cheltenham (2m4f, good) on New Year's Day and on that evidence this 5lb higher mark isn't necessarily beyond him; however, two lesser runs have followed; has to get back on track.
runners.comment: Easy win at Cheltenham in January; mark perhaps not beyond him but return to form needed

runners.name: Peaky Boy
runners.spotlight: Showed abundant promise in late 2024 on his first two chase starts and is on a competitive mark on that form; remains lightly raced and he could improve for last month's run in the Plate at Cheltenham, when finishing mid-division, but others arrive with more compelling claims; a tongue-tie is added.
runners.comment: Could improve for last month's run in the Plate but others have more compelling claims

runners.name: Addragoole
runners.spotlight: Stamina to prove and he failed to threaten when ninth in the Grand Annual last month; however, Cheltenham may not be his track (also not at his best there last October) and he showed improved form to win a competitive handicap at Leopardstown (2m1f, yielding) in December; only seven and there have been hints that this sort of trip may suit; no surprise to see him go well.
runners.comment: Ninth in the Grand Annual but Cheltenham may not be his track; only a 7yo; could go well

runners.name: Eldorado Allen
runners.spotlight: 12yo who staged a renaissance when winning a veterans' chase at Cheltenham in November and has continued in pretty good heart; however, it's likely he'll be vulnerable today; soundly beaten in this race 12 months ago.
runners.comment: Won a veterans' chase at Cheltenham in November but highly likely this 12yo is vulnerable

runners.name: Mahons Glory
runners.spotlight: 10yo who has a career-high mark following easy win in a small field at Huntingdon (2m4f, good to soft); however, he enjoyed himself in front in the Grand Sefton (2m5f, good to soft; fourth) and the Becher (3m2f, soft; fifth) over these fences earlier in the season and can make another bold bid.
runners.comment: 10yo who has a career-high mark, but this front-runner appears to relish these fences
runners.owner: C Nightingale & Hold My Beer Partnership

runners.name: Bill Baxter
runners.spotlight: 10yo who won this in 2023 (soft); he's had his ups and downs since but won at Punchestown (3m, yielding) last May and bumped into a well-handicapped novice when runner-up at Kelso (2m7f, heavy) last time, following wind surgery; could give a good account and be involved.
runners.comment: Won this in 2023 and bumped into a well-treated novice at Kelso latest; could be involved

runners.name: Primoz
runners.spotlight: Exploited a reduced mark to win easily in a small field at Ayr (2m4f, soft) last month and he's also effective on good/good to soft ground; his yard has done very well over these fences but from out of the handicap he's running off a mark 12lb higher than last time, and needs a career best.
runners.comment: Easy win in small field at Ayr last month; from out of the handicap he's 12lb higher today

runners.name: Teddy Blue
runners.spotlight: Can be difficult to settle and he was reluctant to line up at Kempton (2m4f, good) four weeks ago, but that didn't stop him winning; had just a neck to spare but the first two were clear and this 8yo is now 4-10 over fences; further progress is possible and he's not ruled out up just 3lb.
runners.comment: Reluctant to line up at Kempton but it didn't stop him winning, making it 4-10 over fences

runners.name: Gentleman De Mee
runners.spotlight: 10yo who hasn't been at his best this season but it was the same story last term prior to him winning this race (good to soft); he's just 3lb higher than 12 months ago and is capable of playing another leading role.
runners.comment: Below best this season but same story last term prior to him winning this race

runners.name: Coming Up Easy
runners.spotlight: Mistakes costly when pulled up in the Paddy Power Gold Cup at Cheltenham (2m4f, soft) last November when last seen; however, this front-runner was 3-3 in handicap chases previously, the latest win a convincing one at Killarney (2m5f, good to yielding) in August; certainly remains of interest.
runners.comment: Mistakes costly when pulled up at Cheltenham last November; 3-3 in h'cap chases previously

runners.name: Hitman
runners.spotlight: 10yo who has become inconsistent and hasn't shone on his last two starts; however, he has a good record over regulation fences here and has the same mark as when winning the Old Roan (2m4f, good to soft) last October; prominent for a fair way in last season's Grand National; not written off.
runners.comment: Hasn't shone on last two starts but on handy mark; good record over regulation fences here

runners.name: The King Of Prs
runners.spotlight: Won a quality handicap at Fairyhouse (2m1f, soft) last January and he ran okay when eighth in this last year, beaten nearly 19l; however, he's only 4lb lower this time around and has struggled this season.
runners.comment: Near 19l eighth in this last year, when 4lb higher than today; he's struggled this season

runners.name: Pour Les Filles
runners.spotlight: Capitalised on the favourite falling three out to win in small field at Ffos Las (2m3f, soft) in January on stable debut and pulled up/well beaten on both runs since; hard to fancy on the face of it but his yard has an excellent record in this race over the years, so he's not a forlorn hope.
runners.comment: Hard to fancy on the face of it but yard has excellent record in this race over the years

runners.name: Conflated
runners.spotlight: Finished third in the 2023 Cheltenham Gold Cup; showed some spark around Punchestown's banks course this February but this 12yo is nothing like the force of old and was well beaten in the cross-country race at Cheltenham last month; without a win since December 2022 and looks up against it.
runners.comment: 3rd in 2023 Gold Cup; nothing like the force of old & this 12yo is probably up against it

runners.name: OMoore Park
runners.spotlight: 0-11 over fences and 14l behind Madara when sixth of 23 in the Plate at Cheltenham (2m4f, good to soft) last month; however, that was still a respectable run and the fact he represents Willie Mullins, who has won three of the last six runnings and saddles three today, means he's not discounted.
runners.comment: 0-11 over fences; 6th of 23 in the Plate; represents Willie Mullins, so not discounted

runners.name: Il Est Francais
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who is well handicapped on form shown as recently as December 2024, when runner-up in the King George at Kempton (3m, good), and he'll be a thrilling sight over these fences if back on song following wind surgery; however, pulled up on his last two starts and has plenty to prove.
runners.comment: 2024 King George runner-up; wind op since last time but pulled up on his last two starts

runners.name: Croke Park
runners.spotlight: Won two Grade 1 novice chases in December 2024 (2m4f/3m, yielding/good to yielding); he hasn't kicked on this term and was well beaten in a handicap at Leopardstown (2m5f, soft) in February, but getting back on better ground may help and he can go well if tapping back into last season's promise.
runners.comment: Hasn't kicked on this term but can go well if tapping back into last season's promise

runners.name: Soul Icon
runners.spotlight: He's run pretty well in defeat at Kempton on his two starts this year (3m, good/good to soft); also ran okay when ninth in this last year (good to soft) but beaten nearly 20l and he's just 3lb lower this time around.
runners.comment: Ninth in this last year, beaten nearly 20l, and he's just 3lb lower this time around

runners.name: Ile Atlantique
runners.spotlight: His form/jumping have been in and out since his Grade 2 novice chase win at Naas last January,  soundly beaten in a handicap at Leopardstown latest; however, two starts ago he followed Heart Wood home in a 2m7f Grade 3 at Tramore; could have a big say for Willie Mullins and Paul Townend.
runners.comment: Inconsistent but represents Mullins/Townend and he followed Heart Wood home two starts ago

runners.name: Lisnamult Lad
runners.spotlight: Well below his best on both runs this season, the latest at Leopardstown in December; however, he produced a career-best performance when a close second in this 12 months ago (good to soft), pulling clear with Gentleman De Mee, and the return to Aintree could spark a revival; on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Well below best on both runs this season but runner-up in this last year; on the shortlist

runners.name: Indiana Jones
runners.spotlight: He won a Grade 3 novice chase in March 2023 but this 10yo has been nowhere near that level across the last two seasons.
runners.comment: Grade 3 novice chase win in March 2023 but nowhere near that level in last two seasons

races.name: Oddschecker Sefton Novices' Hurdle (Grade 1) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Johnnys Jury
runners.spotlight: Beaten in one run for Nicky Henderson but now 3-3 for current yard after coming from the clouds in the Albert Bartlett at Cheltenham last month (3m, soft); that win seemed unlikely when out the back for much of the race, and even two out he had more in front than behind him; evidently a strong stayer but this flatter, sharper course could ask different questions of him.
runners.comment: Albert Bartlett winner; this flatter, sharper course could ask different questions of him
runners.owner: Blazing Blues Rc Hurlers On The Ditch

runners.name: Mondouiboy
runners.spotlight: Point winner who ran well in defeat in bumpers at top tracks before selling for Â£115,000 at the David Maxwell dispersal sale; they were easy pickings at Ludlow (2m5f) and Ascot (2m3f; again good to soft; favourite both times) but he confirmed himself high class (as his trainer had indicated) when fourth to Johnny's Jury in the Albert Bartlett at Cheltenham, where he was in the firing line throughout and fared best of those who raced prominently; provided that race hasn't left a mark then he's of major interest.
runners.comment: Fared best of those who raced prominently when fourth in the Albert Bartlett

runners.name: Zeus Power
runners.spotlight: After minor wins in Ireland he went off 50-1 for the 2m5f Grade 1 Turners at Cheltenham but he defied those odds to finish third, in spite of so much going wrong during the race (got behind after suffering interference); the manner in which he stayed on offers plenty of encouragement for this extra yardage; it's a nagging concern that only four 5yos have won this since 1988 but intriguing otherwise.
runners.comment: 5yos don't have a great record in this but he was eyecatching in the Turners at Cheltenham

runners.name: Kripticjim
runners.spotlight: Tongue tied the last twice; completed his hat-trick in a 2m4f Grade 2 at Cheltenham on Trials' Day (soft; fine on good) but only sixth in the Albert Bartlett; however, he ran well for a long way in fairness only to wilt up the hill and this flatter track could be a better fit; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Never came up the hill in the Albert Bartlett but ran well for a long way at 22-1

runners.name: Talk To The Man
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner; couldn't win a bumper but he's looked good in winning two novice hurdles, last time justifying market support with an oh-so-easy win under a penalty at Newbury (2m4f, good to soft; runner-up useful); dangerously unexposed and highly rated in a top yard that's also represented by No Drama This End; interesting, and stamina shouldn't be an issue when he's by Walk In The Park and his dam is a sister to Cheltenham Gold Cup winner Lord Windermere.
runners.comment: Has looked promising in winning his two novices and held in high regard at his top yard

runners.name: Dalston Lad
runners.spotlight: He's gone 3-3 since the cheekpieces went on and it was pretty straightforward from the front in a Grade 2 at Haydock the last time (3m, good to soft) when 4l too good for the strong-finishing Ubatuba who ran a respectable seventh in the Albert Bartlett; arrives here fresher than some; respected.
runners.comment: Fresher than some having had a two-month break since his Grade 2 win at Haydock
runners.owner: Cherry Knoll Farm M Ward & D Skelton

runners.name: Harbour Island
runners.spotlight: Dual bumper winner; lacked the gears to mix it with top 2m novices earlier in the season but, raised in distance, he wasn't overly troubled to win minor races at Taunton (2m3f, good to soft) and Ffos Las (2m4f, heavy); mixed messages on pedigree as to whether this far will be a help or a hindrance, but can't be dismissed.
runners.comment: 2m3f/2m4f winner; mixed messages on pedigree as to whether this trip will help or not

runners.name: Good Girl Kathleen
runners.spotlight: It was never in doubt when showing huge improvement in a Grade 3 at Cork (3m, soft to heavy) and when a disappointing favourite for a Grade 2 at Warwick there were apparent excuses; career best needed and by some way, but trainer's reputation speaks for itself; couldn't rule out.
runners.comment: Career best needed and by some way, but trainer's reputation speaks for itself

runners.name: Frankie John
runners.spotlight: Talented Irish raider but with questions to answer at this level having twice finished well beaten in Grade 1s (2m4f/2m6f) since winning a maiden at Leopardstown over Christmas; the cheekpieces are new.
runners.comment: Twice well beaten in Grade 1s (2m4f/2m6f) since his maiden win over Christmas

runners.name: Catchintsavo
runners.spotlight: Progressive novice but this is his first time in a Graded race of any nature and he finished some 10l behind Mondoui'boy at Ascot on his penultimate start; he'd be a surprise winner.
runners.comment: Progressive but finished some 10l behind Mondoui'boy at Ascot in his penultimate race

runners.name: Seaniecon
runners.spotlight: Point winner who was odds-on when winning a maiden at Hereford (3m1f, heavy) and then came up short under a penalty at Uttoxeter (2m4f); this stiffest test yet is likely to prove well beyond him.
runners.comment: Beaten under a penalty last time; this stiffest test yet likely to prove well beyond him

runners.name: Shadow Paddy
runners.spotlight: Has made rapid progress since winning a low-grade handicap at Tipperary last May, last time securing a fourth career win at Listed level at Limerick (2m5f, yielding) in October; has earned his right to a crack at something better but this might be a step too far, especially with stamina to prove.
runners.comment: Has made rapid progress but this might be a step too far, especially with stamina to prove

races.name: Debenhams Handicap Hurdle (Conditional Jockeys' And Amateur Riders' Race) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Gooloogong
runners.spotlight: In good form on AW Flat last spring and returned from an absence for 50-1 win on handicap hurdle debut at Kempton (2m, good to soft) in February; weakened quickly in the Imperial Cup at Sandown (soft) but perhaps a return to less testing ground may help.
runners.comment: Won handicap hurdle debut at Kempton but well beaten in the Imperial Cup since

runners.name: Wandering Ego
runners.spotlight: Second 2m novice hurdle success when comfortably seeing off 11 rivals at Huntingdon 23 days ago; third in a 2m Kempton race (again on good) which has worked out well in his sole handicap; respected for a stable which has had a few run well in this in recent years.
runners.comment: Two novice hurdle wins and he was third in a warm race in his sole handicap; respected

runners.name: Pourquoi Pas Papa
runners.spotlight: Likeable sort who finished out of the first two for the first time but ran as well as he's ever done when fourth of 22 in the Fred Winter (Handicap) at last month's Cheltenham Festival (2m, good to soft; The Mighty Celt sixth); that effort can be marked up slightly as he got messed about a bit turning in; Paul Nicholls won this in 2022 and sent out the second in 2023 and the third (Afadil) in 2024; a must for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Likeable sort who was an excellent fourth in Fred Winter; a must for the shortlist

runners.name: Afadil
runners.spotlight: 18 races and over two years since his last win, albeit still frequently competitive; below par over 2m5f at Ascot only 12 days ago but his peak form has come at around 2m (third in this in 2024).
runners.comment: On a long losing run but often competitive; possibly failed to stay 2m5f latest

runners.name: Captain Ryan Matt
runners.spotlight: Second Tramore success when easily seeing off nine rivals in a 2m1f handicap (soft; first win was on good) on New Year's Day; the handicapper hasn't missed him but there may be more to come for his leading Irish stable.
runners.comment: Handicapper hit him hard for easy Tramore win on New Year's Day but he may do better again

runners.name: Melon
runners.spotlight: Two juvenile hurdle wins last winter; reappeared with a good third of 11 in handicap at Cheltenham (2m1f, good to soft) in December and bounced back quickly from a lesser effort at Windsor when chasing home the reopposing Rubber Ball at Newbury (2m, good to soft) in February.
runners.comment: Placed in two of three handicaps this term, latterly chasing home Rubber Ball at Newbury

runners.name: The Mighty Celt
runners.spotlight: French recruit who has caught the eye on both starts for Dan Skelton, latterly making good late headway from the rear for sixth of 22 in the Fred Winter (Handicap) at Cheltenham (2m, good to soft); could do with learning to settle better but there are bigger performances in him if he can.
runners.comment: Eyecatcher on both starts for Dan Skelton and has bigger performances in him

runners.name: Star Of Guiting
runners.spotlight: Two 1m7f Musselburgh wins this winter; bounced back from a poor run in the Imperial Cup when fifth of 14 back at Musselburgh three weeks ago but that effort suggests the handicapper is winning the argument for now.
runners.comment: Two Musselburgh wins this winter but latest fifth there suggests his mark is about right

runners.name: Sherminator
runners.spotlight: Has won all four completed starts over hurdles (trained by Emma Lavelle for first win); came 6l clear on handicap debut at Exeter (2m, good to soft) last time in the style of one who can keep on improving; considered under Patrick Mullins.
runners.comment: Won all four completed starts over hurdles and probably more to come; considered

runners.name: Harry Lowes
runners.spotlight: Met trouble at a crucial stage when fifth in a warm race on 2m4f Windsor handicap debut in January and made it 2-2 at around 2m when seeing off four rivals in Bangor novice (soft) the following month; remains open to improvement back in a handicap and capable of having a big say for last year's winning trainer/jockey combination.
runners.comment: Progressive; excuse on handicap debut and won his second novice hurdle since; big player

runners.name: Litleangel Duseuil
runners.spotlight: French recruit whose British debut success at Ayr after wind surgery (also tongue-tied first time) seemingly came as a bit of a surprise to connections judged on her 33-1 SP but there was no fluke about it; 7lb higher in a much stronger race now, though.
runners.comment: Ayr stable debut win was a surprise judged on 33-1 SP but there was no fluke about it
runners.owner: Trotters International By A & L McLaren

runners.name: Laafi
runners.spotlight: Very useful on the Flat and has quickly reached a similar level over hurdles, finishing a creditable fifth in strong, big-field handicaps at Leopardstown (yielding) and Kelso (also 2m, good to soft) on his last two outings; each-way claims.
runners.comment: Good fifth in strong handicap hurdles on his last two starts; each-way claims

runners.name: Last Kingdom
runners.spotlight: Won a big-field Listed handicap at Listowel (2m, good to yielding; first-time cheekpieces) for Willie Mullins in September; hasn't got anywhere near that level in three outings without headgear for his new yard in recent months but his mark has dropped 10lb; worth a second look in the betting with a tongue-tie tried now.
runners.comment: Below par in three quick runs for this yard but dropped 10lb; tongue-tie on; check betting

runners.name: No Ordinary Joe
runners.spotlight: On a long losing spell but he was bang there for a long way when fifth in a warm 2m3f handicap at Ascot in February with cheekpieces added; hasn't reproduced that in two subsequent starts but it's possible that a change of headgear (first-time visor now) could give him a lift.
runners.comment: Ascot fifth in February provides hope but held twice since; visor needs to give him a lift

runners.name: Ehteyat
runners.spotlight: Three-time Flat winner; placed in three of four maiden hurdles at up to an extended 2m1f in Ireland this year but doesn't strike as being obviously well treated from an opening hurdle mark in the mid-120s.
runners.comment: Placed in maiden hurdles but opening hurdle mark of 125 looks quite tough

runners.name: Rubber Ball
runners.spotlight: Returned to form with a bang when seeing off the reopposing Melon at Newbury (2m, good to soft) in February; has a quick turnaround as an excuse for a lesser effort in the Imperial Cup at Sandown (2m, soft) one week later, while his trainer reported to the stewards that he'll be suited by a flatter track and a quicker surface, both of which he should get today; not dismissed.
runners.comment: Easily saw off Melon at Newbury and his next run possibly came too soon; not dismissed

runners.name: Charlus
runners.spotlight: No promise in handicaps around 2m5f (Martin Pipe at Cheltenham latterly) since joining Dan Skelton; his hurdle form for Willie Mullins came at around 2m, so it's possible the drop back in trip will help, but the yard's other runners hold more obvious claims.
runners.comment: No promise in 2m5f handicaps since joining Dan Skelton, so others are more obvious

runners.name: Static
runners.spotlight: On a hat-trick after 2m3f wins at Haydock (good to soft) and Bangor (good) under Charlie Maggs last month but the fact he failed to win at around 2m on his first ten starts in Britain hardly boosts confidence in him for this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Last two starts yielded 2m3f wins but 0-10 at around 2m in Britain prior to that

runners.name: Wreckless Eric
runners.spotlight: Returned to form in first-time cheekpieces (retained) when runner-up in the Imperial Cup at Sandown (2m, soft) for the second year in succession; 12 months ago he followed up with a disappointing effort at this meeting but that was over 2m4f and this is more his trip.
runners.comment: Runner-up in last two Imperial Cups at Sandown; claims if showing up here in same form

runners.name: Wild Bill Hickok
runners.spotlight: Won a Listed Hurdle for 3yos at Auteuil (1m7f, heavy) for Noel George & Amanda Zetterholm last April but badly out of sorts when last seen in the autumn; picked up by new connections for 52,000euros in November; a watching brief is the percentage call.
runners.comment: Listed win in France last April but out of form there in the autumn; changed hands since

runners.name: Hamsiyann
runners.spotlight: Justified strong market support in 2m5f Cheltenham handicap (good) in November 2024; belatedly back to that sort of form when second of six at Newcastle (extended 2m4f, good to soft) in November but let down by his jumping when only a respectable fourth at Kempton (2m5f, good) over Christmas; drops back in trip for his first run since.
runners.comment: Best hurdle form at around 2m5f, so this shorter trip is not sure to suit after his break

races.name: Join Racing TV Today Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Where Love Lives
runners.spotlight: February foal by Cotai Glory; Â£55,000 yearling; half-brother to Italian 7f 2yo winner Inbetty; dam 5f/6f 2yo winner (including AW; RPR 88), half-sister to winners Compton Poppy (5f including 2yo) and Bon Port (7f/1m including 2yo), out of maiden half-sister to 5f Group 1 winner Kyllachy; enough to like on breeding and stable 21-192 (11%) with 2yo newcomers in past five seasons; respected, especially if backed.
runners.comment: Enough to like on breeding; stable 21-192 (11%) with 2yo newcomers since 2022; respected

runners.name: Turnstile
runners.spotlight: April foal by Territories; half-brother to winners Pivoting (6f 2yo; RPR 79), U Turn (5f 2yo; 70) and Rotation (6f 2yo; 66); dam placed 5f 2yo (62), out of 5f winning half-sister to 6f Group 1 winner G Force; speedy pedigree offers hope, but he would be a rare winning newcomer from the stable.
runners.comment: Speedy pedigree offers hope, but he would be a rare winning newcomer from the stable

runners.name: Hickos Lad
runners.spotlight: April foal by Mattmu; first foal; dam unraced half-sister to 8.6f winner Host, out of maiden half-sister to Group-placed 7f/1m winner Rare Ransom; already gelded; stable 4-76 (5%) with 2yo newcomers in past five seasons; probably best watched.
runners.comment: Stable 4-76 (5%) with 2yo newcomers in past five seasons; probably best watched

runners.name: Rbflying
runners.spotlight: March foal by Kodi Bear; 70,000euros foal; 70,000gns yearling; half-brother to seven US/Canadian winners including Samurai's Garden (6.5f/1m dirt) and Gardenly (1m dirt); dam 6f winner (RPR 74), half-sister to 5f Group 3 winner Enticing (dam of multiple 7f Group 1 winner One Master); something to like on breeding, but stable 5-188 (3%) with 2yo newcomers since 2022.
runners.comment: Something to like on breeding, but stable 5-188 (3%) with 2yo newcomers since 2022

runners.name: Regal Adventure
runners.spotlight: April foal by Coulsty; 28,000euros yearling; half-brother to Polish 5f 2yo winner My Snow White; dam unplaced 5f-6f 2yo (including AW; RPR 56), closely related to UAE 6f 2yo winner Desert Of Dreams, half-sister to 5f-6f winner Grand Zafeen, out of 7f Group 3 winner; already gelded and stable 1-90 with 2yo newcomers in last five seasons; may be better for the run.
runners.comment: Already gelded and stable 1-90 with 2yo newcomers in last five seasons; better for the run
runners.owner: A Scaife C Scaife G Scaife

runners.name: Hickory Lad
runners.spotlight: March foal by James Garfield; 9,000euros yearling; brother to Qatari 5.5f/6f winner Bluey's Boy, half-brother to winners Assimilation (7f-1m2f AW; RPR 105), Keep On Moving (Swedish 7f) and Princess Park (Hungarian 6f); dam 5f/6f 2yo winner (82); stable 2-20 (10%) with 2yo newcomers since 2022; market helpful.
runners.comment: Stable 2-20 (10%) with 2yo newcomers since 2022; market helpful

runners.name: Thebesthasyetocome
runners.spotlight: February foal by Starman; Â£100,000 yearling; dam placed 7f/1m in France (including 2yo), sister to Hungarian 1m2f winner Polaris Dancer, half-sister to 11.4f/1m4f winner Escarpment, out of French 6f/7f winner; stable 3-52 with 2yo newcomers since 2022, but worth a second look given his purchase price, especially if attracting support.
runners.comment: Stable 3-52 with 2yo newcomers since 2022; worth a second look given his purchase price

runners.name: Too Many Spirits
runners.spotlight: April foal by Rajasinghe; brother to 6f 2yo winner Hougoumont (including AW; RPR 78); dam 5f winner (including 2yo/AW; 84), half-sister to Listed-placed 6f 2yo winner Byzantium, out of 5f winning close relative of useful 5f/6f winner Texas Gold; already gelded; stable 1-14 with 2yo newcomers since 2024; market to guide.
runners.comment: Already gelded; stable 1-14 with 2yo newcomers since 2024; market to guide

races.name: racingtv.com Restricted Novice Stakes (For Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB Race) (Div I)

runners.name: Clonquest
runners.spotlight: Came from well off the pace to finish second at Newcastle (7f) in January and the winner went in again next time; that run sets the standard here and this colt's debut was also encouraging.
runners.comment: Came from well off the pace to finish second at Newcastle (7f) in January

runners.name: Simon Pierre
runners.spotlight: Cost 72,000euros as a 2yo and the maiden he contested at the Lincoln meeting (7f, good to soft) was dominated by well-bred horses from good yards; although tenth of 12, he was beaten only 7.5l.
runners.comment: Although tenth of 12 at Doncaster recently, he was beaten only 7.5l.

runners.name: Arc At Her
runners.spotlight: 20,000gns yearling; sixth foal; half-sister to winners All Go (6f-1m) and Dea Giulia (Italian 9.5f); dam 2m winner (RPR 64) and very well related; the only runner who lacks experience but makes sense to check the betting.
runners.comment: The only runner who lacks experience but makes sense to check the betting
runners.owner: Gee Gees Racing & Hold My Beer

runners.name: Luscious Lil
runners.spotlight: 50-1 and was a remote eighth over 1m at Southwell 20 days ago; one for handicaps.
runners.comment: 50-1 and was a remote eighth over 1m at Southwell 20 days ago; one for handicaps

runners.name: Simbas Pride
runners.spotlight: 2,000gns yearling; beaten under 5l over 5f at Thirsk but that was nearly a year ago and he's up markedly in trip this time; pedigree is quite speedy.
runners.comment: Fourth of ten over 5f on debut nearly a year ago; raised significantly in trip

runners.name: Raja Charger
runners.spotlight: Three heavy defeats have earned him a basement handicap mark; no appeal.
runners.comment: Three heavy defeats have earned him a basement handicap mark; no appeal

runners.name: Mayflower Billy
runners.spotlight: Trailed home last at Chelmsford in August (7f) when 100-1 and cheekpieces are now removed.
runners.comment: Trailed home last at Chelmsford in August (7f) when 100-1 and cheekpieces are now removed

runners.name: My Ballyquinn
runners.spotlight: Ran moderately over 1m at Ripon last year (good to firm; 18-1) but has been gelded ahead of this second season.
runners.comment: Ran moderately over 1m at Ripon last year but has been gelded ahead of this second season

races.name: racingtv.com Restricted Novice Stakes (For Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB Race) (Div II)

runners.name: Supreme Lillia
runners.spotlight: Fair effort when third on 7f Newcastle debut in November but only sixth of seven back there 110 days later; even a return to the debut form likely wouldn't be enough to trouble Bearish.
runners.comment: Third on AW debut last November but only seventh back there four weeks ago

runners.name: Bearish
runners.spotlight: Promising fourth of 15 in 7f Newmarket novice (good) on debut last October; that form has been boosted and he should be very hard to beat on his return to action with improvement likely.
runners.comment: Promise in warm race on last autumn's Newmarket debut; should be hard to beat on return

runners.name: Inferno
runners.spotlight: Both runs on Newcastle's Tapeta last November, leaving 7f debut behind when third over 1m; should be more to come this year.
runners.comment: Promise on second of two AW outings last November; more to come in 2026

runners.name: Pepsea
runners.spotlight: 20,000euros gelded son of Far Above and the second foal of a half-sister to 6f/7f Listed winner Gusto; probably best watched on debut.
runners.comment: Best watched on debut unless the betting strongly hints otherwise

runners.name: Rocklaw
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in a pair of Southwell novices and more one for handicaps.
runners.comment: Well beaten in a pair of Southwell novices and more one for handicaps.

runners.name: Bridgefoot Rambler
runners.spotlight: Beaten 24l when sixth of seven on debut at Ripon (1m2f, good; 80-1) seven months ago and needs to show significant improvement.
runners.comment: Beaten 24l when sixth of seven on Ripon debut last year; significant improvement needed

races.name: Live Streams On Racing TV Extra Handicap

runners.name: Harswell Ruby
runners.spotlight: Had several close calls on turf in the second half of last year; not disgraced back from four months off at Newcastle three weeks ago; 1-22 overall but she could be very competitive with that run behind her.
runners.comment: Not disgraced on AW return and feasibly treated on last year's turf efforts; considered

runners.name: Eves Boy
runners.spotlight: Yet to add to his 2yo novice win but is down to a mark in the 60s for the first time now back on the grass after a two-month break; a return to form wouldn't surprise.
runners.comment: 0-12 in handicaps but down to mark in 60s for first time; return to form no surprise

runners.name: Finally Escaped
runners.spotlight: Unexposed after four AW starts in recent months but never involved when sixth of eight on 1m Southwell handicap debut 13 days ago; has raced keenly and a first-time hood and tongue-tie are reached for now switching to turf; improvement will be needed.
runners.comment: Unexposed after four AW starts but will need improvement switching to turf in headgear

runners.name: Amidst The Chaos
runners.spotlight: Started his handicap career with wins at Doncaster (1m, good to firm) and Musselburgh (1m1f, good) last August; had excuses for his two subsequent defeats (too keen upped to 1m4f final start) and retains unexposed potential; claims if fully primed after six months off.
runners.comment: Won first two handicaps and excuses for two subsequent defeats; claims if ready to roll

runners.name: Mariner
runners.spotlight: Winner over 1m at Doncaster (good to firm) and 1m2f at Ripon (good) last spring; minor late headway on his recent AW reappearance and he's dipped to 1lb below the mark he defied at Ripon.
runners.comment: Should be sharper for recent AW return and now 1lb below his last winning mark

runners.name: Travis
runners.spotlight: Just two wins from his 30 starts but placed over 1m/7f on Newcastle's Tapeta on his last four outings, the latest on reappearance a fortnight ago; successful over 7f here last summer and should go well if stall 12 isn't too big an inconvenience.
runners.comment: Course winner; arrives in form from AW; stall 12 not ideal but should still go well

runners.name: Volto Di Medusa
runners.spotlight: Dual 1m winner (good turf/AW); ran well for third at Wolverhampton last month and shaped quite nicely again (caught too far back) when fifth over 1m2f at Lingfield (AW) a fortnight ago; one of the more likely types.
runners.comment: Dual 1m winner; promise in recent AW starts; one of more likely types

runners.name: Perfidia
runners.spotlight: 1-21 but the win came off 1lb lower last summer and the fact she was a close second on her 2025 reappearance provides hope that she could ready to roll after six months off.
runners.comment: 1lb below her winning mark and went close on last year's reappearance; enters calculations

runners.name: Jamaican Storm
runners.spotlight: Placed twice in March prior to making it 17th time lucky at Newcastle (1m) a fortnight ago; arrives fit and in form but eighth is the best he's managed in four previous turf outings.
runners.comment: Finally came good on AW latest but down the field all four turf starts last year

runners.name: Whiskey Pete
runners.spotlight: Useful for Ralph Beckett but nowhere near that level for this yard last year, albeit placed twice over 1m2f at Doncaster off his reduced mark at the end of last summer; off six months; the betting should help guide to expectations.
runners.comment: Placed off higher marks over 1m2f last year; betting perhaps best guide after 179 days off

runners.name: Jewel Maker
runners.spotlight: Veteran with 12 wins to his name but didn't suggest his next one is near when trailing throughout on his AW reappearance three weeks ago.
runners.comment: This veteran is a 12-time winner but always behind on recent AW reappearance

runners.name: Pol Roger
runners.spotlight: Lost his way in the autumn; has dropped to a favourable mark as a result (went close off 5lb higher last autumn) but a losing run of 21 doesn't inspire confidence; first start for 140 days.
runners.comment: Well treated back from a 140-day break but a losing run of 21 doesn't inspire confidence

runners.name: Copper And Five
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who returns on a favourable mark but this veteran has no great record fresh in recent years.
runners.comment: C&D winner; on favourable mark but this veteran has no great record fresh in recent years
runners.owner: Mrs Sally Doyle And Mrs Ruth Carr

races.name: British EBF Michael Foster Conditions Stakes

runners.name: Mount Athos
runners.spotlight: Running in this for the third time, finishing second in 2023 and 2025; on both occasions he arrived on the back of a 7f win at Kempton and he landed that same race yet again 23 days ago; should give it another good shot.
runners.comment: Second in this in 2023 and 2025; once again arrives after a 7f Kempton win; should go well

runners.name: Scoville
runners.spotlight: Won 1m novices on good to firm on his first two outings, on the second occasion making all by a whopping 14l at Newmarket; didn't confirm that abundant promise in two subsequent Group races (including 5.5l behind Poet Master at Newmarket) but still very early days and could easily resume his progression on this reappearance having been gelded.
runners.comment: Behind Poet Master final run but promise of 14l novice win still fresh in memory; gelded

runners.name: Poet Master
runners.spotlight: Raced almost exclusively at 7f; Group 2 winner in Ireland as a 4yo; ran close to that level when second in Longchamp Group 3 (very soft) and the Group 2 Challenge at Newmarket (good; Scoville 5.5l back in fourth) last autumn; disappointing on his 2025 reappearance but won first time up in 2023 and 2024; leading form claims.
runners.comment: Group 2 winner; second at that level on final 5yo start; goes well fresh; leading claims
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Seacruiser
runners.spotlight: Looked a good prospect when making all in a 1m Newmarket maiden (good to soft) in September 2024 but hasn't been seen since; could be the sort to make up for lost time this year but this is a big ask on only his third start after such a long absence; has been gelded.
runners.comment: Made all in 1m Newmarket maiden in September 2024 but hasn't been seen since

runners.name: Elarak
runners.spotlight: Back on the up with a bang returned to 7f and ridden positively when ready winner of a handicap at Newmarket (good to firm) last September; subsequently gelded; has the potential for even better this year and his stable is no stranger to success in this, including in the Shadwell silks.
runners.comment: Smart effort when ready winner of 7f Newmarket handicap last autumn; likely more to come

runners.name: Myal
runners.spotlight: Most progressive in the last couple of years, winning six 7f handicaps, with the first gained off of a rating of 67; another career-best effort when second in 7f Redcar Listed race (good to soft) on his final 4yo start; should be spot on for this after finishing fifth of 12 in a 6f Doncaster Listed event on his reappearance 13 days ago.
runners.comment: Most progressive at 7f, including Listed second last autumn; sharper for recent 6f return

runners.name: Darkness
runners.spotlight: Useful sort who landed fast-ground handicap wins over C&D and at Goodwood last year; however, he has plenty to find on these terms and failed to beat a rival on his reappearance in 2024 and 2025.
runners.comment: Useful C&D winner but has plenty to finds on these terms

runners.name: Rhoscolyn
runners.spotlight: Last year's Golden Mile win at Goodwood (strong record there) shows he's still smart on his day; third in this in 2022 but hasn't beaten a rival in the last two runnings (all reappearance); safe to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Hasn't beaten a rival in the last two runnings of this and surely up against it

races.name: Download The Raceday Ready App Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Pride Of Donegal
runners.spotlight: Consistent 4yo who has really raised his game since the blinkers went on, twice making all over 1m4f at Wolverhampton; it was never in doubt there last weekend and he's 3lb well in under a penalty; unraced on turf.
runners.comment: Bolted up on the AW last weekend he's 3lb well in under a penalty

runners.name: Asmen Warrior
runners.spotlight: Sixth career run when the odds-on winner for a 1m2f maiden for previous yard; bar one blip, he's been thereabouts in his handicaps for James Owen and this slightly longer trip is worth a go.
runners.comment: Thereabouts in his handicaps for James Owen and this slightly longer trip is worth a go

runners.name: Roarin Success
runners.spotlight: Competitively treated on her form for Harry Charlton and she's not performed badly over hurdles for current yard; had a wind operation; hard to dismiss.
runners.comment: Competitively treated on his form for Harry Charlton; fair form over hurdles for this yard
runners.owner: Hale Racing Lyle Farming Company & Ptnr

runners.name: In The Breeze
runners.spotlight: Won at Pontefract (1m4f, good to soft) in September off just 3lb lower and final three defeats for Mick Appleby were over further; could go well if ready for action after his break.
runners.comment: Returns to his last winning trip and could go well if ready for action after his break

runners.name: Gibside
runners.spotlight: The ground was soft when winning over this far at Catterick in October and he's usually seen over further these days; under these conditions he may be found wanting for some pace.
runners.comment: Often seen over further these days and may now need slower ground at this trip

races.name: Next Meeting Saturday 18th April Book Now Handicap

runners.name: Ey Up Hes A Star
runners.spotlight: Might be better on the AW but shaped encouragingly after a break at Newcastle last month (7f) and he's not had many chances on grass; draw no help.
runners.comment: Might be better on the AW but shaped encouragingly after a break at Newcastle last month

runners.name: Good Karma
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner and has been shaping as though his turn could be near once more, going down narrowly the last twice at Wolverhampton (7f); can race keenly but if getting some cover from stall 4 he should be on the scene.
runners.comment: Can race keenly but if getting some cover from stall 4 he should be on the scene

runners.name: Woodrafff
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner who is fine at 7f or 1m; has been running well for a while and latest effort in the new visor at Newcastle can be upgraded.
runners.comment: Running well for a while and latest effort in the new visor at Newcastle can be upgraded

runners.name: Operation Gimcrack
runners.spotlight: Won two of his four races on the AW this year (at 7f) and has comparable form on turf; wasn't beaten far last month at Newcastle despite losing a shoe.
runners.comment: Won two of his four races on the AW this year and has comparable form on turf

runners.name: Invincible Melody
runners.spotlight: Runner-up eight times but left Stuart Williams for 3,400gns and as a 22-race maiden; ran well when trying this headgear combination once before (early last year) but wouldn't be the percentage call.
runners.comment: Runner-up eight times but left Stuart Williams for 3,400gns and as a 22-race maiden

runners.name: Oselton
runners.spotlight: Sole win and best form has been on the AW and likely best watched back on the grass after a break.
runners.comment: Sole win and best form has been on the AW and likely best watched back on the grass

runners.name: Filey Beach
runners.spotlight: Poor in his last two races but now back with Ruth Carr for whom he won twice over this trip last season; also ran well on his reappearance.
runners.comment: Won twice over this trip last season and also ran well on his reappearance

runners.name: Angel Of England
runners.spotlight: Winless since 2023 and just one effort of note last winter after a lengthy break; had wind surgery during three months away; not wildly appealing.
runners.comment: Winless since 2023; just one effort of note last winter after a lengthy break; had wind op
runners.owner: The Jocks And The Geordies II

runners.name: Blackjack
runners.spotlight: Much more effective on the AW than on grass so recent efforts in that scene are discouraging.
runners.comment: Much more effective on AW than on grass so recent efforts in that scene are discouraging

runners.name: Reidh
runners.spotlight: 2-25 for Richard Fahey, the wins coming in 2024 (one over C&D); left that yard out of form and for 10,000gns; historically well handicapped if Adam Kirby can get him going again; tries cheekpieces; market interesting.
runners.comment: Historically well handicapped if Adam Kirby can get him going again
runners.owner: Jack Nunn Tom Korolkiewicz And Partner

runners.name: Mwafaq
runners.spotlight: Lost his first ten races but won his last two, 7f handicaps at Wolverhampton in October (under Ethan Tindall); seems as effective on grass and just a question of how fit he'll be after his break; mark remains workable; not drawn great.
runners.comment: Lost his first ten races but won his last two, 7f handicaps at Wolverhampton in October
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Jkr Cobbler
runners.spotlight: Running fairly well this year and the handicapper's helped his cause; can sometimes miss the break but if giving himself a chance he might not be far away.
runners.comment: Running fairly well this year and the handicapper's giving him every chance

runners.name: Sunny Orange
runners.spotlight: Hard to win with (4-43) and, although he has form elsewhere, he does like Beverley; took a while to find his form last season.
runners.comment: He does like Beverley and took a while to find his form last season

runners.name: Orbital Chime
runners.spotlight: Two wins over 6f for previous trainers; best run yet for current one (second run in a visor) when not quite seeing out the 8.6f at Wolverhampton six days ago; stall 14 could pose problems but respected nonetheless.
runners.comment: Third last week when not quite seeing out the 8.6f at Wolverhampton

runners.name: Miss Willows
runners.spotlight: Bookended last season with wins, the latest by a short head over this far at Redcar; never won off this high a mark.
runners.comment: Bookended last season with wins but has never won off this high a mark

races.name: Thirsk Hunt Cup Day Saturday 2nd May Book Now Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Juan Les Pins
runners.spotlight: Winner of eight races; fit from the AW when second of ten at Doncaster (5f, good to soft) 12 days ago; 1lb well in and return to 6f and drying ground wouldn't be a problem; high on list.
runners.comment: Eight wins; 1lb well in after recent Doncaster second; 6f and drying ground no problem

runners.name: Stirrup Cup
runners.spotlight: Both wins have come over 6f on soft ground and remains 7lb higher than for the latest; opposable under the likely conditions and the run may be needed; cheekpieces replace blinkers.
runners.comment: Opposable under the likely conditions and the run may be needed; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Belsito
runners.spotlight: Scored twice on a soft surface over 6f at Hamilton last September, but was weak in the market when failing in his hat-trick bid at Pontefract (6f, soft) the following month; may need this first start in six months and wouldn't want the ground to dry out too much.
runners.comment: May need this first start in six months and wouldn't want the ground to dry out too much

runners.name: Barmyblade
runners.spotlight: Won three times last spring and is back off the same mark as for the latest, but he lost his form completely later in the season; proven fresh, though, and not the biggest surprise if he were to figure.
runners.comment: Won three times last spring and is back off the same mark as for the latest; could figure

runners.name: El Pinto
runners.spotlight: Showed ability in both starts on soft ground as a 2yo, but has regressed in an abbreviated career since; best watched for now.
runners.comment: Showed ability in both starts as a 2yo, but has regressed in an abbreviated career since

runners.name: Great
runners.spotlight: Windsor maiden winner (6f, good to firm) for Richard Hannon in May last year and ran well in turf/AW handicaps afterwards, but having been gelded he was well beaten on his stable/seasonal debut at Southwell (7f, AW) last month; enough to prove.
runners.comment: Well beaten on his stable/seasonal debut at Southwell last month; enough to prove

runners.name: Lord Abama
runners.spotlight: Last few efforts have been modest, but all three turf wins have come over C&D on good to firm ground, while is is 3lb lower than for the latest last summer; has won fresh and very much of interest, especially if conditions continue to dry out; stable also runs Macedonian.
runners.comment: All three turf wins have come over C&D on good to firm ground; very much of interest
runners.owner: Mrs K S Pratt & M Dods

runners.name: Marks Choice
runners.spotlight: Best known as a Ripon specialist with ten wins there, including his last two starts of 2025; has won fresh, but has tended to need his reappearance in recent years.
runners.comment: Best known as a Ripon specialist; has tended to need his reappearance in recent years

runners.name: Moonstone Boy
runners.spotlight: Four wins over 5f for Iain Jardine, but unplaced in three starts on the AW since returning for this yard in January; 8lb below last winning mark, but drying ground and return to 6f pose questions; cheekpieces return.
runners.comment: 8lb below last winning mark, but drying ground and return to 6f pose questions

runners.name: Maldevious
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who is 0-6 and was last seen making a quiet stable debut at Southwell (7f, AW) in November; has undergone wind surgery since; unraced on ground faster than good and probably best watched.
runners.comment: 0-6; last seen making a quiet stable debut at Southwell in November; has had a wind op

runners.name: One More Dream
runners.spotlight: Winner of 13 races and just 1lb higher than for the latest, but quiet in two starts at Southwell (6f, AW) since returning in February; needs to step up.
runners.comment: Winner of 13 races, but quiet in two starts since returning in February; needs to step up

runners.name: Macedonian
runners.spotlight: 1-14, the win coming on handicap debut at Kempton (6f, AW) for the Crisfords in March last year; has proved inconsistent for this yard and may need the run; unraced on ground faster than good.
runners.comment: 1-14; has proved inconsistent for this yard and may need the run

runners.name: Hurt You Never
runners.spotlight: Mainly seen over 5f these days, but has won seven times over 6f; ended last season quietly, but is back off her last winning mark and has won after an absence; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Back off her last winning mark and has won after an absence; shortlisted

runners.name: Fiscal Policy
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in all six starts since returning in October, but has dropped to a dangerous mark as a result and he won't have a problem with the likely conditions; off ten weeks, but has a good record fresh; could go well at a price.
runners.comment: Has dropped to a dangerous mark and won't have a problem with the likely conditions
runners.owner: S Thompson Mrs R Carr & G Shields

runners.name: Valentine Catcher
runners.spotlight: Had dropped 9lb below his last winning mark when demoted after passing the post first at Pontefract (6f, good to soft; first-time cheekpieces) last September, but hasn't matched that form since; has won fresh and has been successful on good to firm ground, but a soft surface seems to suit him best; one of three from stable.
runners.comment: Has become well handicapped; has won fresh, but softer ground may suit best

races.name: Bet Boost At bet365 Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Isle Of Sark
runners.spotlight: 2m4f hurdle winner at this track; both wins have come on a sound surface; 0-5 over fences; arrives on a handy mark after being well beaten in an AW race and over hurdles at Southwell in two starts since joining his current trainer; more needed.
runners.comment: 2m4f hurdle winner here; acts on a sound surface; more needed but is on a handy mark.

runners.name: Clonakilty
runners.spotlight: Dual 2m4f chase winner on testing ground; well treated on his form of 12 months ago when with Olly Murphy; needs to be much better than in his three starts so far for this trainer; has had wind surgery since last month's Wetherby appearance.
runners.comment: Dual 2m4f chase winner; needs to be much better than in his three starts for this trainer.

runners.name: Tom Creen
runners.spotlight: Standing dish here with all five career wins having come at the venue and on slow ground, including over this trip; produced one of his better efforts when second here last Boxing Day but not as good since; back on a very handy mark and can go well.
runners.comment: Course specialist; back on a very handy mark after three below par starts; can go well.

runners.name: Cardamon Hill
runners.spotlight: Stays 3m and acts on the forecast sound surface; 50-1, always behind and pulled up on his return from 13 months off the track at Wetherby on latest start; bit to prove.
runners.comment: Pulled up on his return from 13 months off the track at Wetherby latest; bit to prove.

runners.name: Maggies Boy
runners.spotlight: 0-13; well treated on his best hurdles form and stays 3m2f in that sphere; well below par on his last three starts, either side of a break; half-brother won over fences, so may take to this discipline but he has that to prove on his chase debut.
runners.comment: 0-13; well treated on best hurdles form; well below par last three starts; bit to prove.

runners.name: Arctic Saint
runners.spotlight: Placed twice at up to 2m4f on slow ground over hurdles; below par when last seen in that sphere at Warwick last December; has since had wind surgery and is on a competitive mark if he is able to produce his best on his chase debut.
runners.comment: Had wind surgery since latest start; competitive mark if at his best on his chase debut.

runners.name: Paramaribo
runners.spotlight: Point (good) and 2m4f hurdle winner (soft) but has shown no worthwhile form under rules for almost two years and looks best watched.
runners.comment: Has shown no worthwhile form under rules for almost two years and looks best watched.

races.name: bet365 "National Hunt" Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Dartmouth Castle
runners.spotlight: Won a fast-ground bumper here last May; improving over hurdles and beaten just 2l and 1l when second at Ludlow (2m, soft) and Newcastle (2m1f, good to soft) on his last two starts; has the pick of the form here.
runners.comment: Fast-ground bumper winner here; has the pick of the form and is the one to beat.

runners.name: Wendywilldo
runners.spotlight: Soft-ground mares' bumper winner here, when with Danny Brooke; placed twice at around 2m4f over hurdles last season, including on good; not seen for 13 months and has changed stables; tongue-tie goes on; record suggests she might be better for this run.
runners.comment: Soft-ground bumper winner here; second twice over hurdles but may come on for this run.
runners.owner: Miss S J Turner And Mrs J A Brooke

runners.name: Owzthat
runners.spotlight: Showed minor ability in a mares' AW bumper at Newcastle in January 2023; well beaten in two further starts that year; dam a 2m1f and 2m4f hurdle winner; hooded for this first start for her new trainer after a lengthy absence; market can guide.
runners.comment: Minor ability on debut in 2023; back from lengthy absence for new trainer; market check.

runners.name: Whenthemusicstops
runners.spotlight: Didn't show a huge amount in two points; second of five at Plumpton on rules debut (2m4f, good); improved on that before running out of steam at Perth (2m4f, soft) last October when last seen; likely to need another significant step up to win this and a sound surface should help.
runners.comment: Improving at around 2m4f but probably needs another step up to win this.
runners.owner: A Baxter S Sowray K Ward

runners.name: Park Hind
runners.spotlight: Point winner who is 0-20 under rules; pulled up on her last three starts, most recently over fences in February.
runners.comment: 0-20 under rules; pulled up on last three starts, most recently over fences in February.

races.name: Position Payout At bet365 Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Rebel Tribesman
runners.spotlight: Won an Aintree bumper (good) last May; turned over at odds-on when making his hurdles and stable debut over C&D (good) last month, when run out of it after the last; respected but may need to pull out more.
runners.comment: Bumper winner (good); beaten over C&D at odds-on latest; respected but may need more.

runners.name: Kuredu King
