runners.spotlight: Two-time Flat winner at up to 1m6f, including on good, when with the Gosdens (RPR 87); 20-1 and never seriously involved on hurdles and stable debut at Huntingdon (2m, good) last month; since had wind surgery; more needed.
runners.comment: Dual Flat winner; never involved on hurdles debut; since had wind surgery; more needed.

runners.name: Chemistry
runners.spotlight: Won a Listed race (1m6f, good; awarded the race) on the Flat when with Aidan O'Brien (RPR 103); best of five efforts for Ivan Furtado was over 16.5f (AW) at Southwell in December; looks an interesting recruit on his hurdles and stable debut.
runners.comment: Awarded a Listed race (1m6f) on the Flat (RPR 103); interesting recruit on stable debut.
runners.owner: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd

runners.name: Ribble River
runners.spotlight: Three-time Flat winner at up to 1m4f, including on good, when with Kevin Ryan (RPR 75); 66-1 and pulled up (vet reported he had bled from the nose) on his hurdles and stable debut at Doncaster (2m, good to soft) in February; tongue-tie now fitted; bit to prove.
runners.comment: Three-time Flat winner at around 1m4f; pulled up on hurdles debut (bled from the nose).
runners.owner: Hambleton Racing Mrs C Clark Miss A Lee

runners.name: Twilight Safari
runners.spotlight: Second in a four-runner Pontefract maiden (1m2f, good) last July, when with Harry Eustace; well beaten/pulled up in two starts at Wetherby since joining current trainer and going over hurdles; plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Well beaten/pulled up in two starts since joining current trainer; plenty to prove.

races.name: bet365 Handicap Chase

runners.name: Mobile Mamma
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark under rules when jumping well in a Newcastle handicap chase (2m7f, good) 13 months ago; off the track for 348 days prior to her return over that C&D last month, when easy to back and beaten 23l; well treated back on her winning mark and could come on for her reappearance.
runners.comment: 2m7f chase winner (good); may have needed reappearance; well treated off her winning mark.

runners.name: Defence Witness
runners.spotlight: Point, bumper and dual chase winner at up to 3m1f, all on slow ground; below par at Kelso latest (2m5f, good to soft); on a competitive mark if bouncing back but will need to produce better than he has previously done on good going.
runners.comment: Chase winner at up to 3m1f; competitive mark but needs to improve on good-ground form.

runners.name: Goodoldbill
runners.spotlight: Seeking a hat-trick after wins over C&D (soft) and at Newcastle (2m7f, good to soft); up another 5lb for latest success but drying ground not ideal.
runners.comment: Seeking a hat-trick; up 5lb for latest success but drying ground not ideal.

races.name: bet365 Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Wee Alki
runners.spotlight: Back on favoured good ground he pushed Dillarchie all the way here 22 days ago; on these terms there's precious little between them.
runners.comment: Back on favoured good ground he pushed Dillarchie all the way here 22 days ago

runners.name: Dillarchie
runners.spotlight: Repelled Wee Alki here three weeks ago and now has form figures of 1431 over C&D; a 3lb rise shouldn't be too damaging.
runners.comment: Repelled Wee Alki here three weeks ago and now has form figures of 1431 over C&D

runners.name: East Eagle
runners.spotlight: Dual winner who continues to run well and he has form over this C&D; solid chance off current mark.
runners.comment: Dual winner who continues to run well and he has form over this C&D; solid chance
runners.owner: Mrs Janet Cooper & M E Sowersby

runners.name: Celestial Reign
runners.spotlight: Sole win came in a maiden here but over 2m4f; three modest runs in handicaps since returning from a break and has stamina to prove.
runners.comment: Three modest runs in handicaps since returning from a break and has stamina to prove

runners.name: Moro Rock
runners.spotlight: Won over a similar trip at Stratford last summer (on good ground) when only 1lb lower; hasn't kicked on but might be better for a break.
runners.comment: Has won over a similar trip off a similar mark and could return refreshed after a break

runners.name: Star Vantage
runners.spotlight: Wasn't beaten all that far over 3m at Musselburgh last month but that extended his losing run to 32; others preferred.
runners.comment: Not beaten all that far at Musselburgh last month but that extended his losing run to 32

races.name: Best Odds Guaranteed At bet365 Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Clean Getaway
runners.spotlight: Hurdle (including C&D) and chase winner here and won a chase over the equivalent trip here latest, when on the same mark; well treated if able to translate that form back to this sphere; big player.
runners.comment: C&D winner who scored here over fences latest; same mark; big player if showing that form.

runners.name: Blackwater Lilly
runners.spotlight: 2m hurdle winner (good); back to her best when third over C&D (good) last time, from the same mark; thereabouts again.
runners.comment: Back to her best when third over C&D (good) last time; same mark; thereabouts again.

runners.name: Nevershedatear
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner (debut); 0-5 over hurdles but did show improvement at Bangor (2m3f, good; first-time hood) latest; can race freely, so drop in trip may prove positive; has that to prove but she's not written off.
runners.comment: Irish point winner who can race freely; drop in trip may help but she has that to prove.

runners.name: William Yeats
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden bumper here (soft) when with Rebecca Menzies, whom he has left since being a distant third at Wetherby in January (2m, soft); won a fast-ground point, so forecast conditions may help and he looks fairly treated on his handicap/stable debut; needs a market check.
runners.comment: Bumper winner here (soft); fairly treated on handicap debut; needs a market check.

runners.name: Scots Poet
runners.spotlight: C&D winner (good); third on his last three starts, all at around 2m; chance but will need to raise his game to win this.
runners.comment: C&D winner (good); chance but will need to raise his game to win this.

runners.name: King Sacre
runners.spotlight: Didn't show a great deal in his qualifying races but improved significantly when a 9l second over C&D (good) last month; drops 1lb; no surprise if he kicks on again and finishes closer this time.
runners.comment: Improved when 9l second over C&D (good) latest; drops 1lb; no surprise if he kicks on.

runners.name: Justlikefire
runners.spotlight: Peak effort when second at Uttoxeter (2m, soft) last November; still going okay in third when falling two out at Newcastle latest; drops 2lb; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Going okay in third when falling two out at Newcastle latest; drops 2lb; not ruled out.

races.name: Free Bets On attheraces.com Conditions Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Tadej
runners.spotlight: Went close in French Group 2 in July and picked up Deauville Group 3 (also 6f, first-time blinkers) the following month; gelded prior to his reappearance and did enough to show he'd trained on when third of four, beaten 3l, on AW debut at Kempton (1m) in February; wore cheekpieces that day and now drops back to sprinting in returning blinkers; firmly in the picture.
runners.comment: G3 win last year; encouraging return when 3rd on AW debut (1m); player back down in trip

runners.name: Anthelia
runners.spotlight: Splendidly tough juvenile filly who got up late to bag the valuable Super Sprint (5f, good) in July and took her form to a new level when gamely landing the Group 3 Dick Poole at Salisbury (6f, soft) in September; couldn't make a dent in Group 1 Cheveley Park on her final start of the term but that's easily excused and she's a fascinating candidate on this reappearance/AW debut.
runners.comment: Cracking 1st season led to stab at G1 when last seen in October; feared on return/AW debut
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Lxiv Mrs L Millman

runners.name: Ten Carat Harry
runners.spotlight: Five-race winning spree came to an end when third in Lingfield Listed race (7f, Polytrack) in February and again filled that position in valuable event at Newcastle (6f, Tapeta) one week ago; that run can be upgraded (had no cover from a fairly strong wind) and he must be considered with fitness assured.
runners.comment: C&D winner who's fit and having a fine spell; latest can be upgraded; might be the answer

runners.name: Comical Point
runners.spotlight: Highly tried as a juvenile and his best run came on second start when fifth of 15 in Royal Ascot's Norfolk (5f, good to firm) in June; back near that level when third in York Listed event (6f, good) when last seen in October and commands respect on this return and AW debut; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Norfolk fifth who ran well when last seen; respected on return/AW debut; tongue-tie added

runners.name: Sirius A
runners.spotlight: Matched his previous best when neck second (demoted to third) in Group 3 Sirenia at Kempton (6f; Sayidah Hard Spun fifth) in September and took it up a notch when runner-up in warm Listed event at Dundalk (5f, also Polytrack) the following month; ability to act on AW a plus and he's much respected on this return.
runners.comment: Very useful as a juvenile; acts on AW and at 6f; big player if ready for this reappearance

runners.name: Lady Kodiac
runners.spotlight: Made a promising debut on turf (5f) in August and made virtually all when winning her next two starts, both at Chelmsford (Polytrack) over 6f, latterly in October; the least exposed of these and likely we haven't seen the best of her; interesting ahead of this comeback.
runners.comment: 2-2 at 6f on AW; the least exposed of these and she's interesting ahead of this comeback

runners.name: Chairmanfourtimes
runners.spotlight: Consistent at two and his form spiked when winning soft-ground Listed event at Ayr (5f) in September; previously showed he stays 6f and not dismissed on this reappearance/AW debut, albeit easy turf might be more his thing.
runners.comment: Listed soft-ground win last year; that might be his surface but not ruled out on AW debut

races.name: Get The Inside Track With raceday-ready.com Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B-D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Quantum Swift
runners.spotlight: March second foal; half-sister to Qatari 7f winner Neebrook; dam 6f AW winner (including 2yo; RPR 68), out of 7f winner; yard off the mark with its juvenile when landing the Brocklesby; likely player, especially with market support.
runners.comment: Dam 2yo AW winner; yard already off the mark with its juvenile when landing the Brocklesby
runners.owner: C Munro Mrs M Gander And Partner

runners.name: Jolly Good Fellows
runners.spotlight: 20,000euros yearling; March sixth foal; half-sister to 7f AW winner Split Passion (RPR 83); dam 5f and 6f (Listed) 2yo winner (RPR 102), out of 6f 2yo winning half-sister to 5.5f 2yo Group 2 winner Ocean Ridge, is granddaughter of 6f 2yo Group 3 winner; arguably the most interesting of these on pedigree and requires close market inspection.
runners.comment: 20,000euros yearling; probably the most interesting pedigree of these; betting helpful

runners.name: Everatease
runners.spotlight: 15,000gns yearling; March fourth foal; half-brother to 6f/7f winner Rusheen Boy (including 2yo/AW; RPR 70); dam 5f AW winner (52), out of unraced half-sister to 6f winners Magical Max and Koharu; yard always needs respecting with its 2yo newcomers.
runners.comment: 15,000gns yearling; yard always needs respecting with its 2yo newcomers

runners.name: Step To Glory
runners.spotlight: 15,000gns yearling; February foal by Cotai Glory; dam unraced half-sister to 5f Group 3 winner Hurricane Ivor from a family of sprinters; 14-1 but finished last on his debut in the Brocklesby at Doncaster (5f, good to soft) a fortnight ago; now switches to AW and should be capable of better.
runners.comment: 14-1 but last on his debut in the Brocklesby; switches to AW & should be capable of better

runners.name: Mr Macartney
runners.spotlight: 10,000gns February foal; second foal; dam 5f/6f winner (including 2yo/AW; RPR 87), half-sister to winners Dovey Moon (1m) and Rusalka (Italian 7f-8.5f), out of 1m-1m2f winning close relative of HK 6f Group 2 winner Charles The Great; first ever juvenile runner for his promising in-form yard; market check required.
runners.comment: 10,000gns February foal;  first ever juvenile runner for promising in-form yard

runners.name: Thames Waterman
runners.spotlight: 25,000gns yearling; late March third foal; brother to 6f winner Rajaking (including 2yo/AW; RPR 88); dam 6f/7f winner (including AW; 77), closely related to 1m4f winner State Sovereignty, out of maiden half-sister to useful 5f-6f winner Jamaican Bolt; yard can ready one and betting should be helpful.
runners.comment: 25,000gns yearling; yard can ready one and betting should be helpful
runners.owner: Nigel Scandrett Karen Jankel Shane Buy

runners.name: Magical Life
runners.spotlight: Failed to register an RPR when 40-1 and beaten 57l at Bath (turf) on her debut five days ago.
runners.comment: Failed to register an RPR when 40-1 and beaten 57l at Bath (turf) on debut five days ago

races.name: Download The Racecourse App Raceday Ready Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Estissa
runners.spotlight: Dam was a Group/Grade 1 winner for these connections and this one built on promising debut in December when landing C&D novice under Oisin Murphy 18 days ago; fine prospect who looks sure to go on to better things.
runners.comment: Built on promising debut to win C&D novice under Oisin Murphy 18 days ago; fine prospect

runners.name: Royal Poetry
runners.spotlight: From the family of Dubawi; promising third of 13 in 1m Southwell novice on debut in November and she plugged away when dropped sharply in trip for her reappearance here (6f) 11 days ago; step back up in distance an obvious plus and it's highly likely her best days are ahead of her.
runners.comment: Promising debut (1m); 6f too sharp for her recent return; can do better back at this trip
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Calypso Bloom
runners.spotlight: Big prices and well held on both starts so far, latterly at Kempton (1m3f; 250-1) 11 days ago.
runners.comment: Big prices and well held on both starts so far, latterly at Kempton 11 days ago

runners.name: Veld
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to winners Danger Bay (1m1f; RPR 100) and Myna (1m2f AW; 68); dam 1m4f AW winner (86); displayed abundant inexperience when last on her debut at Southwell (1m; 66-1) one month ago; probably needs more time.
runners.comment: 66-1 and looked very raw when last on debut one month ago; likely needs more time
runners.owner: Sir Richard Pilkington & Mrs S Rogers

races.name: Get The Inside Track With raceday-ready.com Handicap

runners.name: Supreme King
runners.spotlight: 0-8 here but completed a 6f hat-trick when scoring at Kempton (Polytrack; also won on Tapeta) in February; repeatedly denied a run when unlucky fifth next time and latest third (Hunky Dory fifth) was a solid effort a fortnight ago; considered.
runners.comment: Completed 6f hat-trick when scoring in February; likely remains in form

runners.name: Lazzar
runners.spotlight: Good spell on AW without headgear this winter, winning Lingfield novice and three Polytrack handicaps (all 6f); cheekpieces were back on when below that form at Chelmsford (raced wide) last time and connections are sticking with them; still not discounted.
runners.comment: Good spell without headgear this winter; below that form in cheekpieces (retained) latest

runners.name: Hunky Dory
runners.spotlight: Took well to first-time cheekpieces when opening his account in December Southwell handicap; spoiled the follow-up attempt by racing too freely and subsequently left Mick Appleby for 30,000gns; had excuses when fair fifth on stable debut at Chelmsford (also 6f; Supreme King third, Lazzar behind) a fortnight ago and might emerge best of that trio this time.
runners.comment: Had excuses on stable debut a fortnight ago; contender to step up on that

runners.name: Addarella
runners.spotlight: Belied 66-1 odds when second on March's stable debut, taking her form figures in 6f handicaps at this track to 1312; way off that when dropped to 5f here a fortnight later but her C&D record cannot be ignored.
runners.comment: 1312 in 6f handicaps here and those form figures cannot be ignored

runners.name: Papa Cocktail
runners.spotlight: Track regular who doubled his tally over C&D when scoring in January and ran respectably in two subsequent starts in this higher grade; sometimes needs luck in the run but he's respected.
runners.comment: Won over C&D in January and been running respectably since; respected

runners.name: Whenthedealinsdone
runners.spotlight: Awarded Lingfield handicap in early 2025 before going 2-2 over this C&D; just four starts since the latest of those wins last March and firmly back on track when edged out at Chelmsford one month ago; 2lb nudge can't be quibbled with and he needs considering.
runners.comment: 2-2 over C&D and he came back to form when second last time; player

runners.name: An Outlaws Grace
runners.spotlight: Last of seven in Lingfield Class 2 (6f, Polytrack) on reappearance and just mid-division when back on turf at Doncaster (7f) 12 days ago; that took his record to 0-9 since debut win but this represents his easiest task for a while; due a further 2lb drop but still needs a second look.
runners.comment: No wins since debut but this is his easiest task since and he's far from dismissed

races.name: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com Handicap

runners.name: Divot
runners.spotlight: Yet to score but has posted some solid efforts since June, including when second over C&D in September; raced freely when disappointing at Southwell the following month but the added hood might help and Oisin Murphy's booking suggests he's ready for this return.
runners.comment: Maiden but was second over C&D in September; hood on with Murphy booked for this return

runners.name: Beaune
runners.spotlight: In good nick since returning from a break, registering a win and two seconds at 1m4f (two classifieds, one handicap) since February; mark is creeping up and wide draw not ideal but he holds solid claims once again.
runners.comment: In good nick over this trip since February; wide draw not ideal but solid claims again

runners.name: Rosco Rogers
runners.spotlight: Maiden who threatened to get off the mark in both starts when wearing cheekpieces in February; bombed out in a newly fitted visor when apprentice-ridden at Southwell last time but needs a second look with the cheekpieces back on.
runners.comment: Cheekpieces were working but he bombed out in visor latest; a possible with CPs back on

runners.name: Late Claim
runners.spotlight: Made successful handicap debut at Lingfield (1m5f, Polytrack) in December and finished runner-up three times since, including over C&D; wheels have come off lately, however, and first-time cheekpieces need to get her firmly back on track.
runners.comment: Wheels have come off lately and new cheekpieces need to get her back on track

runners.name: Everest
runners.spotlight: Went into her last race a 14-race maiden but she'd shown bits and bobs across both codes and got off the mark, staying on strongly to land Lingfield classified (1m4f, Polytrack) a fortnight ago; unexposed in this sphere despite her age and could be a leftfield improver; chance.
runners.comment: Off the mark (1m4f) latest; 7yo mare could become a leftfield improver and go in again
runners.owner: Alan Jamieson Site Services & G L Moore

runners.name: Virtual Hug
runners.spotlight: Landed some tidy bets when taking this in 2024 but subsequently went on a barren spell; finally back on the scoresheet last month but disappointed in a classified (also C&D) one week later; had an excuse that day but others look more solid.
runners.comment: Won this in 2024 and finally scored again on penultime start; others look more solid
runners.owner: Mike Harris Racing & Nikki Evans Racing

runners.name: Versatile
runners.spotlight: 0-11 on AW; nibbled at in the market before blowing the start in 9.5f selling handicap on stable debut here ten days ago; cheekpieces worn that day are dispensed with and he's yet to convince at this trip; still worth a glance at the betting.
runners.comment: Blew the start on stable debut here ten days ago; unconvincing but needs a market check

runners.name: Tiger Beetle
runners.spotlight: Used to stay this sort of trip; very low-key start for this yard but has gradually found his level and was blinkered when third in 9.5f course selling handicap ten days ago; cheekpieces, previously worn without success, are refitted.
runners.comment: Third (9.5f) in blinkers here ten days ago; might be finding his level; cheekpieces return

runners.name: Moon Over The Sea
runners.spotlight: Patchy form since winning at Southwell (1m3f) in January; first-time cheekpieces did no harm when third over that C&D a fortnight ago but goes without them this time; still can't be discounted.
runners.comment: Patchy since winning in January; cheekpieces, worn when third last time, are missing

runners.name: Cogital
runners.spotlight: Won a weak four-runner handicap at Ffos Las (1m6f, good) under David Probert in July but mostly poor form under both codes since; appeared to take a step back in the right direction when second over hurdles last time and Probert is back in the saddle for the first time since that win.
runners.comment: Step back in the right direction over hurdles latest and Probert was aboard for last win

runners.name: Buck Barrow
runners.spotlight: Gained sole win on third start for this yard when staying on at Ffos Las (1m2f, good to soft) in June; some respectable efforts since, including when keeping-on sixth on AW debut at Kempton (1m4f) in November; possibilities if ready for this first start since so watch the market.
runners.comment: Not disgraced on AW debut in November; watch the market on this first start since

races.name: Hotel And Conferencing Wolverhampton Racecourse Handicap

runners.name: Fistral Beach
runners.spotlight: Five-time AW winner who has form figures of 25131 over C&D since January; her last success was in a bunch finish but she responded well to cheekpieces and is only 2lb higher here; respected.
runners.comment: Has record of 25131 over C&D since January; up another 2lb but she should go well again

runners.name: Dayman
runners.spotlight: Overall record of 2-30 but this 7yo has been in good form recently and he was only just caught over C&D last time; only 1lb higher and she should make another bold bid.
runners.comment: Record of 2-30 but she went close off a similar mark over C&D last time; respected

runners.name: Bad Habits
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-26 but he was runner-up behind Romanovich over C&D last month and had another close call at Southwell (7f) 13 days ago; nudged up 1lb but he should be in the thick of things again.
runners.comment: Record of 1-26 but he's been knocking on the door in his last two runs; shortlisted

runners.name: Split Elevens
runners.spotlight: Ten of his 12 wins have come on AW including four at this track (6f/7f); his last success was 14 months ago but that was off today's mark and he wasn't beaten far over C&D last month; dangerous.
runners.comment: Prolific AW winner who is on last winning mark and wasn't beaten far over C&D last time

runners.name: Romanovich
runners.spotlight: Five-time turf winner who made it 2-20 on AW when beating Bad Habits over C&D 18 days ago; 3lb higher from wide draw here but he still looks on a workable mark and is tactically versatile; in the mix.
runners.comment: Beat Bad Habits when winning over C&D latest; up 3lb but he's in the mix again
runners.owner: Mrs S M Maine & Roger Gould

runners.name: Cooramook
runners.spotlight: 0-14 for Harry Eustace but she had some close calls (6f and 7f) along the way; on dangerous mark but she made a low-key start for new yard here (6f) in February and needs to leave that form well behind; first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: On dangerous mark but she's 0-14 and was laboured on stable debut here in February

runners.name: Amelias Joy
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the tenth attempt with a narrow win over C&D last month; only 2lb higher here but she was disappointing when favourite at Southwell last time and needs to step up again after that.
runners.comment: Made the breakthrough over C&D last month but he was well held at Southwell last time

runners.name: Court Of Session
runners.spotlight: Five-time AW winner who scored over C&D in January but he was well below form at Southwell two weeks ago; bit to prove again after that but he still looks feasibly treated and has possibilities if he can bounce back.
runners.comment: Well below form at Southwell last time but has claims if he can bounce back

runners.name: Harbour Vision
runners.spotlight: 11-time course winner, including three since December; only 1lb higher than when completing a C&D double in February but he has struggled on his last four starts; needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: 11-time course winner but he's struggled since his C&D win in February; needs to revive

runners.name: Tilani
runners.spotlight: Won at the Galway festival (8.3f, soft) last August but he showed regressive form in his final four runs for Dermot Weld; returns after 171 days off and has something to prove on his AW/stable debut; equipment removed.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish 4yo who is untried on AW and has something to prove on his stable debut

runners.name: Present Times
runners.spotlight: 1m AW winner for Charlie Appleby as a 2yo but he's struggled in five runs for his current yard; now 19lb lower than for his opening mark but he has plenty to prove on this drop back in trip; headgear removed.
runners.comment: Mark is tumbling but he's struggled for current and has plenty to prove back in trip

runners.name: Thapa Vc
runners.spotlight: 13-time winner including four over C&D; has mixed record since his Bath win last September but he ended last year with a creditable fourth at Newcastle (7f, Tapeta); 1lb lower on his return and has possibilities if this sets up for his closing style.
runners.comment: 13-time winner and his last success was off 1lb higher; dangerous on his return

races.name: Download The Raceday Ready App Handicap

runners.name: Tactical Blitz
runners.spotlight: Has improved with each of his four starts and he showed a good attitude when justifying support on handicap debut at Newcastle (6f, Tapeta; first run since gelded) last month; still looks feasibly treated off 3lb higher and he shaped as though this step back up in trip could suit last time; big player with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Won on nursery debut at Newcastle and he's a big player again back up in trip; headgear on

runners.name: Pints In Peace
runners.spotlight: 0-9 when trained in Ireland but he won a C&D maiden on stable debut last month and backed that up with a creditable third in a Kempton handicap (7f) last week; on same mark here and he should give it another good shot.
runners.comment: Won over C&D on stable debut and was third in a Kempton handicap last week; in the mix

runners.name: Orange Emperor
runners.spotlight: No progress in his three 2yo runs but he returned after a gelding operation with a near-miss on handicap debut at Kempton (6f, Polytrack) 11 days ago; 2lb well in off the same mark here and he's open to more progress at this new trip; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Had a near-miss at Kempton last month and he's 2lb well in off the same mark; interesting

runners.name: Calling A Star
runners.spotlight: Delivered on her 2yo promise when making all in a maiden here (6f) on her return in February; has 2lb rise to deal with back in a handicap but she was as good as ever last time and is open to more progress back up in trip; respected.
runners.comment: Made all in a maiden here in February and she's respected back in a 7f handicap
runners.owner: A Smith J Henwood & Star Amusements

runners.name: Dublin Bay
runners.spotlight: Dual turf winner (6f) last summer and he's run respectably from the front at Kempton (7f, Polytrack) in his last two starts; should benefit from his reappearance run last month and he's still unexposed at this trip; dangerous with cheekpieces reapplied.
runners.comment: Dual turf winner who has run respectably at Kempton (7f) in his last two runs; dangerous

runners.name: Born A Star
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f Musselburgh maiden (good) last August; had tough task in valuable sales race at York the following month but he was well held again when last of four on handicap debut here (8.6f; first run since gelded) last month; may have needed that after a break but he has some questions to answer.
runners.comment: Musselburgh winner but he was last of four on handicap debut here (8.6f) last month

runners.name: Deluded
runners.spotlight: The maiden he won over C&D last September was a weak one and he found a York sales race too hot to handle one month later; gelded during his absence but he looks on a tough mark for handicap debut and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Won a C&D maiden last autumn but he looks on a tough mark for handicap debut; been gelded

races.name: Book To Stay Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap

runners.name: Three Socks On
runners.spotlight: Improved form on handicap debut when finishing well to force a dead-heat at Chelmsford (1m, Polytrack; 3-1) in January; only 1lb higher on his return and the rival he shared the prize with last time has form figures 2221235 since then; key player.
runners.comment: Forced a dead-heat on handicap debut at Chelmsford and he's only 1lb higher; key player

runners.name: Adba
runners.spotlight: Well held in three AW runs (1m) since November but this unexposed filly has a good pedigree and she's a possible improver upped in trip on handicap debut; needs close look with headgear added.
runners.comment: Unexposed filly and she's a possible improver upped in trip on handicap debut; headgear on

runners.name: Just Call Me Mo
runners.spotlight: Struggled in his three 2yo runs and was always behind on handicap debut over C&D (weak 7-1 shot) last month; may have needed that run but he needs to show a lot more this time.
runners.comment: Always behind on handicap debut over C&D last month and others are preferred

runners.name: Always A Saint
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to four winners at up to 1m2f including Limato (6f/7f including AW/Group 1; RPR 126); no impact in his qualifying runs but he made some late headway over 1m on Polytrack in final two starts last year; been gelded since and he looks interesting now upped in trip on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Well-related gelding and he looks interesting upped in trip on handicap debut

runners.name: Al Amirah
runners.spotlight: Beaten 9l or more at big prices in all four starts including on handicap debut over C&D (sixth of nine) last month; still early days but she needs a transformation.
runners.comment: Beaten 9l or more in all four starts including on handicap debut over C&D last month;

runners.name: Smoker Bellamy
runners.spotlight: Well held in all four runs but he made some late headway after stumbling on his nursery debut at Kempton (1m, Polytrack; 9-2) last August; returns after a lengthy absence but he has some stamina in his good pedigree and needs watching in market at this new trip.
runners.comment: Returns after a lengthy absence but he needs watching in market at this new trip

runners.name: Let Her Go
runners.spotlight: Some promise in novice/maiden events but she started her handicap career with a laboured effort over 1m at Chelmsford (sixth of seven; 16-1) in February; this new trip could help on pedigree but she needs to leave that latest form well behind.
runners.comment: Lightly raced filly but she was laboured on handicap debut at Chelmsford (1m) in February

runners.name: Make It Up
runners.spotlight: Has a smart pedigree but he was beaten 21l, 13l and 20l in his three runs in the autumn; now goes handicapping off a basement mark but he still needs major improvement after 168 days off; been gelded.
runners.comment: Didn't show much as a 2yo and he needs major improvement on handicap debut; been gelded

runners.name: Rajbello
runners.spotlight: Well held seventh in his first two runs (7f/8.6f) before a remote fourth in a Kempton novice (7f) in December; now goes handicapping with blinkers added but he needs improvement after 114 days off.
runners.comment: Handicap newcomer but he needs improvement with blinkers added after a break

races.name: Connolly's RED MILLS Irish EBF Auction Maiden Hurdle (IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Dunleer Crystal
runners.spotlight: Rated 114 and kindly treated by race conditions; standout effort a 2.25l second behind Los Blanco at Down Royal (2m1f, yielding); testing ground may not have suited at Naas on latest; half-brother to a winner at this trip and big chance back on a sounder surface.
runners.comment: Rated 114, well treated by race conditions, testing ground excuse latest, big chance

runners.name: Ifallgoeswell
runners.spotlight: Top rated here at 116 and plenty of placed efforts in bumpers and maiden hurdles; not a bad run on handicap debut at Cork five days ago and should be on the premises back in a maiden, although has to give weight away to all.
runners.comment: Top rated at 116, okay run on h'cap bow latest, in the mix back in a maiden

runners.name: All Night Revival
runners.spotlight: Went close in a Fairyhouse bumper on Rules' debut; decent efforts in 2m maiden hurdles at Gowran (heavy) and back at Fairyhouse (heavy) the last twice; needs to find improvement for this longer trip and quicker ground to win.
runners.comment: Went close in a bumper, respectable 2m maiden runs last twice, moves up in trip

runners.name: De Jour En Jour
runners.spotlight: Has run okay in three maiden hurdles at 2m1f-2m7f on testing ground without really threatening to win; rated 105 and needs to find a bit more improvement to get his head in front; pulled up twice on quicker ground in points, which is a concern.
runners.comment: Rated 105, respectable runs in maidens on testing ground, quicker conditions a query

runners.name: Great Entirely
runners.spotlight: Pulled up on sole point start in December; can only be watched on Rules' debut.
runners.comment: Pulled up on sole point start in December, can only be watched for now

runners.name: The First Turn
runners.spotlight: Well backed and duly won a Thurles bumper (15.5f, gd-yld) on debut in October; always behind when beating just one rival home at Leopardstown in December and off since; bred to make a hurdler and in good hands so not dismissed.
runners.comment: Well backed for debut bumper win, poor latest but bred for this game, not dismissed

runners.name: Nameyourgame
runners.spotlight: Placed in one of two points but beaten 48l on hurdles debut at Clonmel two weeks ago; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Placed in one of two points, beaten 48l on hurdles debut, others appeal more

runners.name: Poets Duke
runners.spotlight: Sent off at 6-1 on debut at Naas (2m, sft-hvy) and while he only finished seventh, it wasn't a run devoid of promise; pedigree suggests he'll improve for better ground and he's an interesting runner for local yard.
runners.comment: Debut run not devoid of promise, bred to act on better ground, interesting for local yard

runners.name: Miss Donna
runners.spotlight: Has run to similar RPRs in the mid-80s in a bumper and two maiden hurdles; much more needed.
runners.comment: Similar level of form in a bumper and two maiden hurdles, much more needed

races.name: Wexfordwalkingtrails.ie (C & G) Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Captains Speech
runners.spotlight: Point winner found just Royal Gladiator too good on hurdles debut at Cork (2m4f, yielding) when the 2-1 joint favourite and the fourth from that race won a maiden at Cork last weekend; leading claims.
runners.comment: Point winner, excellent second on Rules' bow at Cork, leading chance

runners.name: Le Moulin Rouge
runners.spotlight: Promising third behind stablemate on debut here (16.5f, yielding) in October, shaping like this trip would suit; ran a blinder upped to 20.5f at Leopardstown (gd-yld) in December behind Lazare De Star (Royal Gladiator 3.25l behind); locally trained and leading chance on form.
runners.comment: Promise here on debut, fine run at Leopardstown latest, leading chance for local yard

runners.name: Green Angel
runners.spotlight: Solid fourth in a 2m1f Down Royal maiden hurdle (yielding) in December; below that level upped to this trip on soft at Ayr in February but was still a decent run; needs to find a bit more to get his head in front.
runners.comment: Promise in both starts, needs to find a bit more to get his head in front

runners.name: Tinto De Verano
runners.spotlight: First reserve; not bad efforts in first two bumpers, here (yielding) and at Leopardstown (gd-yld), but no show on soft on latest in another bumper at Thurles; return to better ground a plus for hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Promise in first two bumpers inc' here, ground will suit for hurdles debut; reserve

runners.name: Sir Topham Hatt
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; wide margin wins in small field points at Ballygogue House and Liscarroll on last two starts, both on soft; clearly has some level of ability and dam a sister to Lord Windermere; could go well if gets a run.
runners.comment: Easy point wins last twice, dam a sister to Lord Windermere, could go well; reserve

runners.name: King Maxios
runners.spotlight: Maxios gelding; dam 9.4f AW winner (RPR 61), half-sister to useful 1m4f winner Sayadaw, out of 1m2f winning sister to high-class 1m-1m2f performer Johann Quatz and half-sister to French Derby winner Hernando; tongue-tie on for debut; watch.
runners.comment: Maxios gelding, dam 9.4f AW winner, tongue tied for debut, best watched

runners.name: Seekthisfairland
runners.spotlight: Quest For Peace gelding; half-brother to point/2m1f hurdle winner Captain Cool; dam point winner, half-sister to 2m-2m2f hurdle/chase winner Tryptonic, out of dual 2m hurdle winner; best watched on debut unless the market speaks.
runners.comment: Quest For Peace gelding, half-brother to 2m-2m1f winner, watch unless market speaks
runners.owner: Kevin Sexton & James McIlwee & James Convery & M G Byrne

runners.name: Pilgrims Walk
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; always out the back in maiden hurdles at Cork and Limerick; may find his level in handicaps.
runners.comment: Never involved in either maiden hurdle run, will find level in handicaps; reserve

runners.name: Davitts Quay
runners.spotlight: 150-1 and always near the back on debut at Clonmel; may need more time.
runners.comment: 150-1 and never sighted on debut at Clonmel, may need more time

runners.name: Silly Mongoose
runners.spotlight: Modest efforts in a trio of bumpers at 2m-2m2f; can only be watched on first start over timber.
runners.comment: Modest form in three bumpers, can only be watched on first start over timber

runners.name: Nebraska Brave
runners.spotlight: Triple-figure odds and has run accordingly in three maiden hurdles at 2m-2m2f; hood now reached for.
runners.comment: No impact at huge prices in three maiden hurdles, hood now reached for

runners.name: Calm Flight Ahead
runners.spotlight: Unlikely winner based on the evidence of his three previous maiden hurdle efforts.
runners.comment: Zero impact in three previous maidens, unlikely winner
runners.owner: Miss Phidelma Elvin & Donal P Keane

runners.name: Cornaroya Rocco
runners.spotlight: Tailed off on debut in Galway bumper and pulled up on hurdles debut at Fairyhouse when a 300-1 shot; put a line through.
runners.comment: Tailed off in a bumper, pulled up  in a Fairyhouse maiden hurdle, put a line through

runners.name: Sams Favourite
runners.spotlight: Refused at the second on debut at Leopardstown; miles behind Captains Speech on sole completed start at Cork; look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Miles behind Captains Speech on sole completed start at Cork, look elsewhere

runners.name: Imarajan
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose maiden for Eddie and Patrick Harty; similar RPRs (low 90s) when fifth at Gowran and fourth at Fairyhouse; needs more on debut for new yard after a break.
runners.comment: Dual-purpose maiden, okay runs in both maiden hurdles, needs more on stable debut

races.name: Visitwexford.ie Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Cowper Hall
runners.spotlight: Won off 14lb lower on last run over timber at Fairyhouse in December; last two runs in chases, unseating at Tramore and well beaten at Thurles; could rebound returned to this discipline.
runners.comment: Raised 14lb for F'house win, below best in chases last twice, could rebound in this sphere

runners.name: Accustomed
runners.spotlight: Point winner; built on promise of a solid run at Leopardstown in December when narrowly denied at Down Royal (20.5f, yielding) last month and the sixth from that race has won since; should be bang there again off 3lb higher with Kennedy booked.
runners.comment: Narrowly denied at Down Royal latest, form been boosted, big player off 3lb higher

runners.name: Hitthehayson
runners.spotlight: Back to back chase wins at Fairyhouse (3m, heavy) and Haydock (2m4f, gd-sft) last November-December; rated failed to exploit his lower hurdles mark back over timber at Navan in February and off since; others preferred.
runners.comment: Back to back chase wins in Nov'-Dec', failed to exploit lower hurdles mark latest

runners.name: Ceroc
runners.spotlight: Has won twice over hurdles, including here over 3m, but losing run mounting and was pulled up in a Clonmel novices' handicap chase on latest; others arrive in better form.
runners.comment: Course winner at 3m but losing run mounting, pulled up in chase latest, others preferred

runners.name: Great Bird
runners.spotlight: Sound efforts in first two handicaps and held every chance when falling at the last at Thurles in October (22.5f, gd-yld); below best the last twice but could rebound dropped in trip and returned to better ground; cheekpieces on.
runners.comment: Has shown winning potential, below best last twice but could rebound; cheekpieces

runners.name: Miss Mini Bee
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner has been running respectably over hurdles; finished a 4.75l third of 13 at Gowran (2m4f, heavy) last month; same mark here but quicker ground a query.
runners.comment: Running respectably over hurdles, placed on heavy latest, quicker ground a query

runners.name: Noble Talent
runners.spotlight: Struggled in chases in first three runs this term and similar story back over hurdles at Cork on latest; has slipped below his last winning mark but not many other positives.
runners.comment: Struggled this season inc' back over hurdles latest, below last winning mark the only plus

runners.name: Sean Mc
runners.spotlight: Ran excellent races when runner up at Fairyhouse (2m4f, soft) and Punchestown (19.5f, sft-hvy); trip may have stretched him when well beaten latest but he's unproven on better ground; chance if he acts on it (half-brother won on good).
runners.comment: In good form before trip may have stretched latest, chance if acts on better ground

runners.name: Bocellis Voice
runners.spotlight: Won his beginners' chase here in April 2024 but no joy since and he was pulled up when last seen in a Down Royal handicap chase in February; new headgear combination needs to spark.
runners.comment: Course winner over fences, out of form in recent starts, new headgear combo tried

runners.name: Annies Belle
runners.spotlight: Handicap debutante has hinted at promise in maidens at 2m-2m2f; hood added to tongue tie and two of yard's last four runners have won; could go well.
runners.comment: H'cap debutante, hinted at promise in maidens, hood on, yard in form could go well

runners.name: Teenage Kiss
runners.spotlight: Has hinted like his turn is near the last twice; kept on well for a 2.25l fourth over 2m3f at Naas on latest so this extra furlong could eke out more and he's gone well here before; leading chance.
runners.comment: Hinted like turn is near last twice, this trip should suit, ran well here before, chance

runners.name: Ersudaly
runners.spotlight: Point winner (on good to yielding) didn't show a whole lot in three maiden hurdles at 2m-3m on soft ground; hooded for her handicap debut, this ground will suit and worth checking in the market.
runners.comment: Point winner, no show in maidens, hood on for handicap debut, ground should suit

runners.name: Stumblin In
runners.spotlight: First reserve; lost form after winning at Tramore last April; off since a poor run at that venue last August; likely best watched on return.
runners.comment: Lost way after Tramore win last April, off since August, best watched on return; reserve

runners.name: Takarengo
runners.spotlight: No win since 2021 and while he has occasionally run well in defeat, his comeback run on the Flat at Navan last month was poor; should strip fitter but hard to fancy.
runners.comment: No win since 2021, poor on Flat latest, should be fitter but hard to fancy

runners.name: Glendine Billy
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; 0-12 over hurdles; has a couple of placed efforts to his name but hard to fancy on recent showings; cheekpieces tried last time discarded.
runners.comment: 0-12 over hurdles, hard to fancy on recent showings, cheekpieces left off; reserve

runners.name: Speakasyoufind
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; placed in a point but generally poor form over hurdle; pulled up on chase debut at Fairyhouse on latest in January; cheekpieces tried now.
runners.comment: 0-7 over hurdles, pulled up on chase debut latest, cheekpieces reached for; reserve

runners.name: Humble Brag
runners.spotlight: Modest form in maidens and his fourth on handicap debut at Clonmel (2m, heavy) was a remote one; pedigree suggests this longer trip will suit and it's still early days for this well bred gelding; dropped 1lb and not discounted.
runners.comment: Remote fourth on 2m h'cap debut, bred to stay this far, still early days, not discounted

races.name: Irish Injured Jockeys Rated Novice Hurdle

runners.name: Rockys Howya
runners.spotlight: Prolific pointer is on a four-timer after notching up wins in claiming hurdles at Limerick (2m5f, heavy) and Naas (2m6f, sft-hvy) and a handicap chase at Limerick (2m7f, heavy); has to prove his effectiveness on better ground, however.
runners.comment: Prolific pointer, won last three on testing ground, not sure sounder surface will suit

runners.name: Long Branch
runners.spotlight: Point winner gained first win under Rules in a Thurles novice (22.5f, yld-sft) in December; 5.5l third under a penalty back there on his next start and unseated when on the retreat on handicap debut at Down Royal on latest; 3m no problem but needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Point winner, Thurles novice win in Dec', beaten when unseating latest, must bounce back

runners.name: Walks The Talk
runners.spotlight: Form figures of 1521 on ground with good in the description, the fifth coming in a listed mares' hurdle at Haydock; floundered on heavy in a Gowran Grade 3 on latest but return to better ground a huge plus; should go well.
runners.comment: Struggled on heavy in Gr' 3 latest but solid form on better ground, should go well

runners.name: Agameoftwohalves
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the fourth attempt in maiden hurdles, scoring by 2l at Punchestown (2m3f, sft-hvy) in February; steps up markedly in trip now and unproven on better ground (half-brother won on good); chance if he stays (mixed messages in pedigree).
runners.comment: Punchestown maiden hurdle winner on latest, big step up in trip, chance if he stays

runners.name: Jisco Du Brem
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner (on good ground) is 0-4 over hurdles and rated 105; kept on for a 17l third of 13 at Thurles (2m7f, sft-hvy) on handicap debut latest; return to better ground a positive but needs more to take this.
runners.comment: Good ground bumper winner, 13l third on h'cap debut latest, more needed to win this

races.name: Tomcoole Farm Novice Handicap Chase

runners.name: James The Brave
runners.spotlight: Killarney handicap winner in October (2m7f, yielding) and took advantage of lower hurdles mark at Punchestown in February before third in a similar event; well-backed favourite back chasing last month, disputing lead (clear of remainder) when unseating two out at Limerick (2m2f, heavy); remains very much of interest off 2lb higher mark.
runners.comment: Every chance when unseating two out at Limerick; big player if avoiding serious error

runners.name: Duffys Hodey
runners.spotlight: Decent performer at a modest enough level, three hurdle wins and twice successful over fences off lower marks this term in Limerick handicaps, most recently last month when patiently-ridden to lead two out for ready 11l win (2m4f, soft); up 9lb but remains 10lb lower than hurdles so remains very much of interest.
runners.comment: Up 9lb for recent Limerick win but still nicely treated compared to hurdles mark

runners.name: Three In A Row
runners.spotlight: Winning hurdler; 0-11 chasing but rated 3lb superior in this discipline after runner-up finishes at Punchestown (2m4f, yielding to soft) and Naas (2m4f, heavy); recent 2m1f Navan handicap fourth wasn't a bad effort, this more his trip so one to consider.
runners.comment: Recent Navan handicap fourth wasn't a bad effort, this more his trip so one to consider

runners.name: Ballybrittas
runners.spotlight: Winning point-to-pointer and hurdler; runner-up on first two attempts chasing, in beginners' at Thurles (2m5f) and Clonmel (2m1f) last season, absent since tame effort at Roscommon (good) in June; probably best watched.
runners.comment: Absent since tame effort at Roscommon in June; probably best watched

runners.name: Mahons Way
runners.spotlight: Grade 3-placed hurdler nowhere near that level in four chase starts, okay effort in 2m4f Naas beginners'; absent since disappointing favourite on handicap debut over 2m at Tipperary last July, fading tamely from last after racing up with pace; trip more in his favour on comeback and still scope for improvement off 12lb lower chase mark.
runners.comment: Yet to prove himself chasing but 12lb lower than hurdles; absent since July

runners.name: Choccabloc
runners.spotlight: 128-rated hurdler for Nicky Henderson; remote second in 2m6f Fairyhouse novice his best run over fences for current yard, most recently pulled up after never travelling in 2m5f Fairyhouse handicap on Saturday; hard to fancy unless quicker ground brings serious improvement.
runners.comment: Pulled up at Fairyhouse on Saturday; quicker ground will need to bring lots of improvement

runners.name: Park Of Kings
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdles winner; beaten a long way in three beginners' chases this term, most recently never counted on handicap debut in Naas Listed contest in February; hurdles form on better ground so these improved conditions could see improvement (10lb lower than over hurdles).
runners.comment: Recent handicap debut leaves him with lots to find but better ground should suit

runners.name: Tequila Talkin
runners.spotlight: Highlight of eight runs over hurdles when winning C&D maiden last July (good); has struggled in handicaps, testing conditions at Leopardstown probably not suiting last twice; straight into handicap company for chase debut and likely best watched.
runners.comment: C&D maiden hurdle winner; straight into handicap company for chase debut

runners.name: Ho My Lord
runners.spotlight: No success yet over fences and is beginning to look well exposed, the addition of cheekpieces (replaced here by blinkers) failing to bring improvement the last twice; declining mark but hard to fancy, although better ground in his favour.
runners.comment: 0-12 chasing; declining mark but hard to fancy, although better ground in his favour

races.name: BOYLE Sports Supporting Irish Racing Veterans Handicap Chase

runners.name: Supreme Jet
runners.spotlight: Better hurdler but has performed respectably in beginners' chases this season, runner-up at Cork (2m, yielding) and led most of the way when fourth at Down Royal (2m4f yielding); solid first handicap effort at Limerick last month, having travelled well into the race; could go well.
runners.comment: Solid first handicap effort at Limerick last month, could go well

runners.name: Bythesametoken
runners.spotlight: Winner here over hurdles and third chase win came in 2m2f Listowel handicap chase last September (off 116) after more than a year's absence; another good comeback run at Haydock in February, not so good at Kelso a fortnight later over shorter trip; this probably the limit of his stamina.
runners.comment: This probably the limit of his stamina but place claims

runners.name: Dont Go Yet
runners.spotlight: Dual chase winner who got off the mark over hurdles here last month in decisive fashion, going clear on the run-in over 2m (soft); second in this race last year (Krabat third) off 7lb higher so obvious claims (1lb wrong).
runners.comment: Winning hurdler here last month; last year's second with obvious claims off 7lb lower mark

runners.name: A Wave Of The Sea
runners.spotlight: Fine servant won a Grade 1 juvenile hurdle and most recent successes came in 2m4f Kilbeggan handicap chases last year; penultimate Thurles fourth his best recent effort, stamina test probably beyond him in valuable 2m6f Leopardstown handicap latest; more suitable trip/ground here and drops in grade.
runners.comment: Trip/ground here and drops in grade; contender

runners.name: Farmers Lodge
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 10yo won Fairyhouse beginners chase in early 2024 and later same year back-to-back wins in handicap hurdles at Fairyhouse and Punchestown; absent since pulled up in 2m6f Fairyhouse handicap hurdle a year ago; unexposed on only fourth chase start, good fourth on only previous handicap run (off 130); interesting but may need this.
runners.comment: Absent a year but still unexposed over fences; interesting

runners.name: Flash De Touzaine
runners.spotlight: Gained the most recent of his three chase wins in a 2m3f handicap on good ground at Ballinrobe last May; lacks a recent run but trip/ground in his favour so local challenger respected.
runners.comment: Lacks a recent run but trip/ground in his favour so local challenger respected
runners.owner: J Carthy & Eamon Nolan & Kieran P Ryan & Mrs Avril Doyle

runners.name: Digby
runners.spotlight: Sole chase win came in 3m Bangor handicap a year ago, following month added 2m7f Ballinrobe handicap hurdle; way below best this season, most recently pulled up after never travelling over inaedquate 2m here last month; again this trip on the short side.
runners.comment: Best form over further, opposable on recent form

runners.name: Caesar Rock
runners.spotlight: Veteran with modest strike rate including 2m6f Tramore chase last summer on good; comes here in decent form though, most recently second to Krabat in 2m4f Limerick handicap last month after a break, having every chance; better ground here could see him turn the tables on that rival (1lb wrong).
runners.comment: Recent second to Krabat at Limerick; better ground could see him turn the tables

runners.name: Krabat
runners.spotlight: In good form this season, gaining second Limerick win last month, coming late under patient ride to win going away (2m4f) on preferred heavy ground; nudged up to near career-high mark now and quicker ground a slight concern.
runners.comment: Recent Limerick winner on preferred heavy; near career-high mark now and quicker ground

runners.name: Brideswell Lad
runners.spotlight: Six-time chase winner (including here) but not since May 2024; missed 2025 entirely and hasn't convinced since January return over both hurdles and fences.
runners.comment: Yet to convince since January return from long absence

runners.name: Watch The Weather
runners.spotlight: Won over fences last season, over C&D (good) and on soft at Punchestown (2m6f) off this mark; form earlier this season not so good, absent since never counting in Munster National in October; noteworthy jockey booking on return to action.
runners.comment: Form earlier this season below best but noteworthy jockey booking on return to action

runners.name: Union Park
runners.spotlight: Better chaser than hurdler, although hasn't won since taking 2m4f Cork handicap hurdle in November 2023; safely held over hurdles of late, of limited appeal back chasing.
runners.comment: Safely held over hurdles of late, of limited appeal back chasing

runners.name: General Clermont
runners.spotlight: Reserve; better chaser than hurdler, although hasn't won since scoring over C&D in August 2024 off 8lb lower mark, standout run when close second over 2m6f at Listowel in September; soft ground probably against him at Limerick following month and has been absent since; 4lb wrong.
runners.comment: Long absent but trip/ground in his favour; 4lb wrong if getting in; reserve

runners.name: The Folkes Tiara
runners.spotlight: Both chase wins hae been in quite valuable 2m1f handicaps (yielding), most recently at Fairyhouse in November 2024; below best this season, beaten nearly 20l over same trip at Leopardstown over Christmas on latest; cheekpieces now added to usual tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Below best this season, cheekpieces now added to usual tongue-tie

runners.name: Fakir dOudairies
runners.spotlight: Four-time Grade 1 chase winner (peaked at 167) for Joseph O'Brien but left yard after a disappointing cross-country debut in December 2024; just okay efforts back on cross-country circuit of late, at Punchestown and when not looking to stay 3m6f at Cheltenham last month; back over the conventional fences here and hard to assess (trip/ground to suit) but handicapper giving him every chance.
runners.comment: Grade 1 winner hasn't convinced at cross-country hard to assess now; trip/ground to suit

runners.name: Fighting Fit
runners.spotlight: Better chaser, although last win came over 2m1f at Listowel festival in 2023 (off 132); encouraging hurdles return at Galway in October but hasn't built on that in three starts since; back chasing here off considerably higher mark so claims not obvious but yard going well.
runners.comment: Below form hurdling since October return; back chasing off much higher mark here

races.name: Racing Again 7th May Handicap Chase

runners.name: My Good Pal
runners.spotlight: Won 2m4f handicap hurdle here in August under today's regular rider and some decent placings afterwards; unseated on chase debut last year when in front three from home at Downpatrick but didn't feature subsequently on softer ground at Ballinrobe; unexposed on return to fences and better ground could suit.
runners.comment: Unexposed on return to fences and better ground could suit

runners.name: Warm In Gorey
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner over hurdles, 0-17 chasing but C&D second last summer off 5lb higher; has failed to complete last three outings so very hard to be confident in.
runners.comment: C&D second last summer off 5lb higher but has failed to complete last three outings

runners.name: Finnians Row
runners.spotlight: C&D winner last August off this mark (good); pulled up on last month's comeback here on soft but entitled to come on for that and ground coming in his favour.
runners.comment: C&D winner off this mark last summer; better for recent comeback run and ground to suit

runners.name: Rockstown Girl
runners.spotlight: Was gaining her third career hurdles win when returning to form at Down Royal last month, taking advantage of reduced mark and drop in grade over 2m5f (Rajjnatin third); 0-5 chasing, though was going okay when brought down three from home in 2m5f Fairyhouse handicap; 3lb lower here so respected.
runners.comment: Recent hurdles winner relatively unexposed chasing; respected

runners.name: Baltinglass Hill
runners.spotlight: Three-time winning hurdler is 0-13 chasing, pulled up after some indifferent jumping last time; back from a break, handicapper has given him every chance and better ground in his favour.
runners.comment: Back from a break, handicapper has given him every chance and better ground to suit

runners.name: Connemara Shore
runners.spotlight: Winning pointer with placed hurdles form and off the mark chasing at the second attempt in C&D handicap last month in first-time blinkers, showing good attitude to shrug off a strong favourite (pair clear of third-placed Butty O'Brien); 7lb rise looks fine, big chance of a follow-up win if handling quicker conditions.
runners.comment: Clear of third-placed Butty O'Brien in C&D win last month; big chance of follow-up win

runners.name: Malton Groove
runners.spotlight: Long standing maiden runner-up in this race last year off 95 (yielding); hasn't been shaping as though his turn is near in recent starts, pulled up at Down Royal over Christmas; handicapper giving him every chance though.
runners.comment: Last year's second not in same kind of form of late but now 6lb lower

runners.name: Butty O Brien
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner; 0-5 chasing but mild encouragement last month over C&D when modest enough third to Connemara Shore (soft); much more needed and cheekpieces tried.
runners.comment: Fair C&D third last month mildly encouraging; much more needed and cheekpieces tried

runners.name: Rajjnatin
runners.spotlight: Modest form in maidens and handicaps for Evanna McCutcheon, more encouraging yard debut in 2m5f Down Royal handicap hurdle last month when well backed, finishing third to Rockstown Girl; meets that rival on same terms here on chase debut.
runners.comment: Down Royal hurdle third to Rockstown Girl on yard debut; same terms switched to fences now

runners.name: Patsys Pride
runners.spotlight: First reserve; yet to trouble the judge over hurdles and on recent chase debut when ran okay in 3m Clonmel novice handicap without looking to stay; drop in trip may suit and better ground.
runners.comment: May have not stayed 3m on recent chase debut; drop in trip may suit here; reserve

runners.name: Person Of Interest
runners.spotlight: Won over this trip at Downpatrick last summer but recent form leaves him with plenty to find, never travelling last time at Plumpton; visor now replaces cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Recent form leaves him with plenty to find, visor now replaces cheekpieces

runners.name: Nolans Rocco
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner got off the mark under rules last month in 2m7f Limerick handicap chase despite hanging left late on; 6lb rise probably workable although different ground conditions now.
runners.comment: Recent Limerick winner; 6lb rise probably workable although different ground conditions

runners.name: Oogum Boogum
runners.spotlight: 18-race maiden hurdler has run okay here in the past; debuts over fences in handicap company and inexperience a likely negative.
runners.comment: 18-race maiden hurdler; lack of previous experience a negative here on chase debut

runners.name: North Of Nashville
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; sole win came here in 3m1f handicap in 2023 (yielding); very lightly raced since, soft/heavy probably against her of late; potential improver back on better ground.
runners.comment: 3m1f winner here in the past; potential improver back on better ground; reserve

runners.name: Barely Made It
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced mare a maiden in both point-to-points and under rules for James Grace; yard debut and market may guide.
runners.comment: Lightly raced mare a maiden in points and under rules; yard debut and market may guide

runners.name: Spread The Faith
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; 0-7 over hurdles and yet to trouble the judge, pulled up last twice; new headgear combination tried for chase debut but very hard to be confident in.
runners.comment: 0-7 over hurdles; headgear on for chase debut but very hard to be confident in; reserve

runners.name: Elusive Prince
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner was unable to make the breakthrough under rules for both Henry de Bromhead and Jimmy Barcoe; debuts for new yard and hard to fancy off this mark.
runners.comment: 0-15 for previous yards; debuts for new yard and hard to fancy

races.name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Maiden

runners.name: New Yorker
runners.spotlight: No Nay Never colt is the second foal out of the Lowther/Cheveley Park/Flying Five winner Fairyland; dam (by Kodiac) a sister to useful 5f/6f winner Atletico, half-sister to Australian 7f Group 2 winner Now Or Never, out of unraced half-sister to  Group 1 winner Dream Ahead.
runners.comment: By No Nay Never, second foal out of dual Group 1 winner Fairyland, of automatic interest
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Matriarchal
runners.spotlight: A 45,000gns yearling buyback; fourth foal; half-sister by Sioux Nation to winners in Australia and Italy; dam French winner, half-sister to Group 2 winner Loxley.
runners.comment: Half-sister by Sioux Nation to winners in Australia and I dam won in France

runners.name: Belle Island
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to four winners; dam 5f 2yo winner; beaten a shade over 4l when second here on debut two weeks ago; open to improvement.
runners.comment: Beaten a shade over 4l when second here on debut, open to improvement
runners.owner: Gary Devlin & N W T Dalzell

runners.name: Amigos Legacy
runners.spotlight: First foal; gelded son of Earthlight; dam; by Iffraaj, unplaced, half-sister to winners including the useful Barenboim (six races) and, Ambiente Amigo (1m2f Listed); respected stable, but not typically associated with juvenile winners.
runners.comment: Stable much respected at this venue, but not typically associated with juvenile winners

runners.name: Litmus Test
runners.spotlight: 160,000gns Y; fourth foal; half-brother by Havana Grey to winners Niigata (1m2f AW; RPR 84) and Maremma (Swedish 6f/1m); dam 6f winner (64), sister to 7f Listed winner Intense Pink, out of half-sister to 6f Listed winner Paradise Isle.
runners.comment: Cost 160,000gns as a yearling, half-brother by Havana Grey to winners, top stable

runners.name: Ranting Duke
runners.spotlight: Â£35,000 Y; third foal; dam Listed-placed 6f 2yo winner (RPR 91), closely related to 6f winner Hightime Heroine, half-sister to Listed winners Redolent (1m) and Pepita (7f), out of 1m winner.
runners.comment: Â£35,000 yearling; third foal, dam Listed-placed 6f 2yo winner, others preferred

races.name: DundalkStadium.com Handicap

runners.name: Oxford Circus
runners.spotlight: Expensive yearling; unraced for the Crisfords, bought cheaply last October; narrow winner of a C&D maiden in February on third start; subsequently stripped of that victory due to her rider being deemed ineligible on a technicality; dropped away to finish in rear on handicap debut over C&D three weeks ago.
runners.comment: Narrow winner of a C&D maiden in February (lost race on a technicality) in rear on latest

runners.name: Sovereign Banter
runners.spotlight: C&D winner last October, beating good yardstick Nikki Swango; has made the frame in four of his six subsequent races here, including two seconds; best of three 2026 runs when third behind Winemaker in January; stable's horses showing good form across a range of categories.
runners.comment: Third behind Winemaker here in January; stable in great form; can make his presence felt

runners.name: Winemaker
runners.spotlight: Recorded a fifth win (third on AW) when scoring over C&D in January off 5lb lower (has won off a mark of 72 before); hampered when attempting to follow up over 7f next time, finishing 2 1/2l fourth; not at his best back on turf at the Curragh before another solid 7f run here two weeks ago.
runners.comment: Solid 7f run here two weeks ago, has a slight edge over Hot To Foxtrot

runners.name: Rattletheonionbag
runners.spotlight: A 30-race maiden; placed in six of her 17 races at this venue; generally uninspiring form lately, although could be given some sort of chance on her third placing over C&D three starts ago.
runners.comment: A 30-race maiden; placed in six of her 17 races at this venue, uninspiring form lately

runners.name: Son Of Wind
runners.spotlight: 1-15; well enough handicapped on juvenile form when he achieved a peak rating of 70 based on consistent form at Chelmsford; regressive last season; no sign of a revival in form when last of 14 on Irish debut over 7f here two weeks ago.
runners.comment: No sign of a revival in form when last of 14 on Irish debut over 7f here two weeks ago

runners.name: Snowballs
runners.spotlight: Glimmers of promise in maidens but has struggled in three handicaps over 5f; made some late headway after starting slowly when beaten 7l in seventh behind An Laochmor here nine days ago.
runners.comment: Beaten 7l in seventh behind An Laochmor here nine days ago, unlikely to reverse form

runners.name: Cooee Call
runners.spotlight: Weak form for his previous trainer; marginally better when sixth of ten behind today's rival An Laochmor on stable debut here last week.
runners.comment: Sixth of ten behind today's rival An Laochmor on stable debut last week, well held

runners.name: Hot To Foxtrot
runners.spotlight: Very little mileage on the clock; definite promise in two of her maiden runs, the first of which was in 2023; three handicap attempts in 2025 were poor; much improved when fifth over 7f here a fortnight ago, but has a bit to find with third-placed Winemaker.
runners.comment: Much improved when fifth over 7f here a fortnight ago but has a bit to find with Winemaker

runners.name: Jered Maddox
runners.spotlight: 12-86, including nine course wins (four over C&D); mark has dropped significantly since his last win in 2025; generally poor form, until an encouraging display in taking third over 5f here three weeks ago; rider reported he received a check in the final furlong.
runners.comment: Prolific winner at this venue, generally poor form before encouraging display last time

runners.name: Rough Diamond
runners.spotlight: C&D winner, five-time scorer in all; has struggled in three 7f runs since joining John Carr after a spell in Britain.
runners.comment: Has struggled in three 7f runs since joining John Carr after a spell in Britain

races.name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Maiden

runners.name: Allsortz
runners.spotlight: Best of five turf runs last season when fourth in a decent 6f Fairyhouse contest in June; handled this surface when a fair third over C&D in November; second here nine days ago; good form claims but vulnerable to a newcomer of Hinder's likely calibre.
runners.comment: Placed over C&D on his last two starts, good form claims but possibly vulnerable

runners.name: Real Encounter
runners.spotlight: A 150,000euros breeze-up buy; 16-1 on debut at Leopardstown, managed a fair fourth of 14 having been outpaced at a crucial stage; met some trouble in running when fourth behind the useful-looking Yellowstone Lake here in September; runner-up Emmeleia has franked the form here recently; should feature now.
runners.comment: Made the frame in maidens at Leopardstown and this venue last year, should feature now

runners.name: Hinder
runners.spotlight: Noteworthy as a half-sister by Expert Eye to Babouche (6f inc 2yo Group 1; RPR 117), Zarinsk (6f-1m inc 2yo/Group 2); half-sister to four others winners; dam 7f and 1m (Listed) winner (103).
runners.comment: Half-sister to two high-class performers for this stable, good chance of a winning debut

runners.name: Coasting
runners.spotlight: 20,000gns yearling; Bated Breath gelding is the first foal out of an Elusive Quality mare who ran once; stablemate of the twice-placed Allsortz who is much more likely to feature.
runners.comment: Stablemate of the twice-placed Allsortz who is much more likely to feature

runners.name: Beach Barbie
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; by Footstepsinthesand out of Magic Bear, a 5f/6f winner (including at this venue) for this stable; dam is from the family that produced the trainer's Group 1-winning sprinter Slade Power; likely to find a few of her younger rivals too good.
runners.comment: Dam a 5f/6f winner for this stable, likely to find a few of her younger rivals too good
runners.owner: N W T Dalzell & Edward Lynam

runners.name: Aughlish
runners.spotlight: Got into the money (promoted to third) here six weeks ago on debut; this race appears stronger.
runners.comment: Got into the money here six weeks ago on debut, this race appears stronger

runners.name: Meriden
runners.spotlight: Failed to get into contention at the Curragh on debut; likely to need more experience.
runners.comment: Failed to get into contention at the Curragh on debut, likely to need more experience

runners.name: Atallah
runners.spotlight: Lost ground at the start and finished in the rear at Fairyhouse on only start last season.
runners.comment: Lost ground at the start and finished in the rear at Fairyhouse on only start last season

races.name: Irishinjuredjockeys.com Handicap

runners.name: Royal County Glory
runners.spotlight: Solid 6f form at this venue under today's rider, notably a staying-on second in a nursery last November; fourth of 14 the following month after being squeezed for room two furlongs down; steps up in trip after a creditable third here last week on his first 2026 outing; could score with just a little improvement.
runners.comment: Has made the frame four times over 6f at this venue, needs just a little improvement

runners.name: Queen Leila
runners.spotlight: Second over C&D in January when well fancied to improve on her moderate 1m nursery form; reportedly got upset in the stalls when out of the money here in February, no apparent excuse last time; confidence dented by that run, but worth a market check.
runners.comment: Second over C&D in January three starts ago, well fancied then, worth a market check now

runners.name: Imperial Fighter
runners.spotlight: Two minor wins on the Flat in Britain; failed to build on a second placing over 1m here in January; best of four subsequent runs when fourth in a claimer; down the field over hurdles last time.
runners.comment: Two minor in Britain; failed to build on a second placing over 1m here in January

runners.name: Macadams Rock
runners.spotlight: Four-time C&D winner, latest win 12 months ago; a long way below his best on last two visits.
runners.comment: Four C&D wins, the latest in this race last year, a long way below best on last two visits

runners.name: Methgal
runners.spotlight: C&D winner two weeks ago, capable of featuring again off a 4lb higher mark.
runners.comment: C&D winner two weeks ago, capable of featuring again off a 4lb higher mark

runners.name: Cosmic Force
runners.spotlight: A 14-race maiden; placed only once (in a small-field race for George Boughey); best form in 2025 was in claimers; not beaten far in sixth over 6f here last month; same placing when 2 3/4l behind Methgal over C&D two weeks ago; place possibility.
runners.comment: Not beaten far in sixth in his two runs here this year, needs to reverse form with Methgal

runners.name: Brosna Green
runners.spotlight: No success in 16 starts, 12 of those at this venue; fair fourth in a 1m fillies' maiden here in January; hard to fancy on the balance of form.
runners.comment: Fair fourth in a 1m fillies' maiden here in January, hard to fancy on the balance of form

runners.name: Ferrari Desert
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in October 2024 and twice second here in the opening months of 2025; probably need the run here in January and should be close to peak fitness now after a recent Curragh run.
runners.comment: Much more effective here than on turf, potentially interesting after a recent Curragh run

runners.name: Caoilte
runners.spotlight: Best of his three 1m outings at this venue came on debut; hard to assess, the market may help to clarify his prospects on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Hard to assess, the market may help to clarify his prospects on handicap debut.

runners.name: Will Be King
runners.spotlight: Won twice at this venue for Joseph O'Brien early in his career (2019 and 2020); absent throughout 2023 and 2024; did well on his reappearance over an extended 1m at Wolverhampton last month, ran prominently all the way and beat all bar the favourite; strong contender if replicating that form.
runners.comment: Returned from a very long absence with Wolverhampton second, should go close

runners.name: Straight Back Up
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in ten starts in Britain; has finished in front of only two of 21 rivals in his last three starts; jockey booking is the only positive
runners.comment: Unplaced in ten starts in Britain, poor form lately, jockey booking is the only positive

runners.name: Rodeeve
runners.spotlight: Placed only once on turf, soundly beaten twice at this venue; lightly raced last year, best of her three runs when seventh of 14 over 1m at Thurles.
runners.comment: Placed only once on turf, soundly beaten twice at this venue, lightly raced last year

races.name: BAR 1 Betting Grand National Extra Places Maiden

runners.name: County Carlow
runners.spotlight: 170,000gns yearling; strong even-money favourite when runner-up here three weeks ago (7f) and his inexperience was very evident; looks sure to step up on that form.
runners.comment: Inexperience evident when a beaten favourite here three weeks ago; good chance

runners.name: Grand Premiere
runners.spotlight: $260,000USD yearling; only 7-2 for his C&D debut in November and would probably have won had he been that bit wiser; promising.
runners.comment: 7-2 for his C&D debut in November and would probably have won had he been that bit wiser
runners.owner: Fergus Galvin & John P Wade & Marc Detampel

runners.name: Eniac
runners.spotlight: Improved on his second run last season when third over 1m at Leopardstown (RPR 72); Irish Derby entrant who probably needs further than this but clearly brings potential into his second campaign.
runners.comment: Third over 1m at Leopardstown and brings potential into his second campaign

runners.name: Golddream
runners.spotlight: Ran okay for a 50-1 chance on his recent debut here over 7f, though never threatened the principals; held by County Carlow.
runners.comment: Fair debut effort here over 7f but this has the look of a stronger maiden

runners.name: Concaire
runners.spotlight: 17,500gns yearling; second foal; dam unraced sister to 1m2f Listed winner Extremis; immediate fitting of headgear is rather unnerving.
runners.comment: 17,500gns yearling; immediate fitting of headgear is rather unnerving

runners.name: Scientific
runners.spotlight: 6-1 chance when beaten 18l at the Curragh last month (1m, heavy; held up); this different surface might well see him in a better light.
runners.comment: The ground was heavy when beaten 18l into seventh at the Curragh last month
runners.owner: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith

runners.name: Police Gazette
runners.spotlight: Brother to US 5f/5.5f turf winner Stony Point; dam French/US 8.5f-9.5f winner who was Listed class; cheekpieces fitted for debut and looks the yard's second string.
runners.comment: Cheekpieces fitted for debut and looks the yard's second string

runners.name: Amrum
runners.spotlight: Frankel colt who is a brother to 1m3f AW winner Byblos (RPR 89) and half-brother to top-class 6f-1m performer Siskin; lots to like on paper.
