runners.comment: Frankel colt who is a half-brother to top-class 6f-1m performer Siskin

runners.name: Threebiggulps
runners.spotlight: 5,000euros yearling; dam third 1m/1m2f AW (RPR 71), half-sister to winners Mammas Girl (7f Group 3) and Master Of War (6f 2yo Listed); already gelded and wouldn't be the obvious answer.
runners.comment: 5,000euros yearling; already gelded and wouldn't be the obvious answer

runners.name: Kalir
runners.spotlight: 2yo form nothing to shout about but he's in the Irish 2,000 Guineas and likely to make a better 3yo.
runners.comment: 2yo form nothing to shout about but in the Irish Guineas and likely to make a better 3yo
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

races.name: View Restaurant Summer Offer Handicap

runners.name: Sarmiento Power
runners.spotlight: C&D win in September off 11lb lower; only fifth to Desert Friend two runs back and was well beaten over 1m4f here last week.
runners.comment: Only fifth to Desert Friend two runs back and was well beaten over 1m4f here last week

runners.name: Jawhary
runners.spotlight: Won twice over C&D during the winter and ran well three weeks ago in a higher grade; possible.
runners.comment: Won twice over C&D during the winter and ran well three weeks ago in a higher grade

runners.name: Celtic Druid
runners.spotlight: Back from a break to win over C&D a month ago when nicely on top late on; Sadhbh Tormey rides again off a 5lb higher mark; respected.
runners.comment: Back from a break to win over C&D a month ago when nicely on top late on; up 5lb

runners.name: Palazzo Persico
runners.spotlight: Three AW wins when trained by Ed Dunlop; 0-5 for current yard but was placed over 1m2f here last month; 1m too sharp last time; each-way claims if on his game.
runners.comment: Placed over 1m2f here last month; 1m too sharp last time; each-way claims if on his game

runners.name: Al What
runners.spotlight: No wins in seven and beaten a good way in all her four handicaps (at about 1m2f); off a reduced mark and cheekpieces are tried, but risky for now.
runners.comment: No wins in seven and beaten a good way in all her four handicaps (at about 1m2f)
runners.owner: Mrs M Cahill & Mrs Caroline Hendron & Peter Robin Parkhill

runners.name: Doctor Grace
runners.spotlight: Has won here and regularly placed; however, recent efforts have been below par and was behind Jawhary last time.
runners.comment: Recent efforts have been below par and was behind Jawhary last time

runners.name: Slowdownbarney
runners.spotlight: Only 1-23, the win coming over 1m6f at Wolverhampton; did go off favourite here two weeks ago and a lack of pace after starting slowly wasn't ideal (behind Desert Friend); had previously hinted at a return to form.
runners.comment: Promising run two starts back and not a lot went right here two weeks ago

runners.name: Fine Print
runners.spotlight: Won twice at Wolverhampton a year ago (1m6f/2m); lacks a run and hasn't been pulling up any trees over hurdles.
runners.comment: Dual AW winner but lacks a run and hasn't been pulling up any trees over hurdles

runners.name: Desert Friend
runners.spotlight: Had been finishing behind some of these but was sporting a tongue-tie for the first time on the AW when staying on best over 2m two weeks ago; he's 4lb higher and back in trip, which asks a bit of him.
runners.comment: Recent course winner but he's 4lb higher and back in trip, which asks a bit of him

runners.name: Expert Analysis
runners.spotlight: 0-4 over hurdles and 0-8 on the Flat; well held over 1m2f here in November when tried in a visor.
runners.comment: 0-12; well held over 1m2f here in November when tried in a visor

runners.name: Grey Fable
runners.spotlight: Poor strike-rate of 2-34 and both wins at Chester over an extended 1m4f in July 2024; struggled last year and best watched now back from a break.
runners.comment: Dual Chester winner in 2024; struggled last year and best watched now back from a break

runners.name: Mephisto
runners.spotlight: Completed a hat-trick in handicap hurdles last summer before losing his way; effective here and not badly treated, so could easily stage a revival.
runners.comment: Effective here and not badly treated, so could easily stage a revival

races.name: Greyhound Racing Every Weekend Handicap

runners.name: Ramair
runners.spotlight: Third on handicap debut here but didn't progress when behind Keepsgettingbetter three weeks ago; has his work cut out to reverse those placings.
runners.comment: Behind Keepsgettingbetter three weeks ago; has his work cut out to reverse those placings

runners.name: Elm Creek
runners.spotlight: First run of much note when third of 13 on handicap debut at Navan 11 days ago (1m2f, heavy; first-time cheekpieces); he was beaten over 7l but appears to have found his level.
runners.comment: Beaten about 7l into third on handicap debut but appears to have found his level

runners.name: Keepsgettingbetter
runners.spotlight: Nothing last season and didn't appear overly fancied for her handicap debut here three weeks ago (1m; 12-1), but she responded well for pressure to lead late on; raised 7lb but she is now at least a winner and promises to stay this far.
runners.comment: Responded well for pressure to win here over 1m on handicap debut; goes further
runners.owner: Mrs Richard Henry & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Sea Of Dubka
runners.spotlight: Similar profile to Musashi in that she showed precious little last season (at about 1m) but now goes into handicaps for a respected yard; makes sense to let the betting guide.
runners.comment: Now goes into handicaps for a respected yard; makes sense to let the betting guide
runners.owner: Anthony Head & Stephen E McCarthy & Mrs Paul Shanahan

runners.name: Cameclose
runners.spotlight: Posted modest RPRs in this three maidens (7f/1m) but this son of Camelot should at least approve of this longer trip on his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Modest in maidens but by Camelot so longer trip could assist on handicap debut

runners.name: Musashi
runners.spotlight: Beat just a total of two horses in his maidens last season, at 1m, 7f and 6f; however, top trainer has had him gelded and fits a visor for handicap debut; market could be informative.
runners.comment: Desperate last season but has been gelded and visor fitted for this handicap debut

races.name: Maker's Mark Mile Stakes (Grade 1) (Turf)

runners.name: Notable Speech
runners.spotlight: High-class miler, winner of four Group or Grade 1s, including Woodbine Mile and Breeders' Cup Mile at Del Mar (by nearly 2l, again accelerating sharply) on final two starts of 2025; better than these at his peak but the margin on comeback (he needed race on 4yo debut) is thin, given late-running style.
runners.comment: Winner of four at top level, notably 2025 Breeders' Cup Mile; hard to beat if at his peak

runners.name: Aomori City
runners.spotlight: Group 2 winner at Goodwood as juvenile; very useful form at best since, including when about 1l second to Quddwah in Group 2 Zabeel Mile at Meydan this January; ran poorly in Abu Dhabi last time; no record of front-running, but that may be about to change.
runners.comment: Group 2 winner as juvenile and very useful form at best since; poor effort latest

runners.name: Rhetorical
runners.spotlight: Successful in five of first six starts, notably Grade 1 Coolmore Turf Mile here last October by just under 1l (got first run, held on well) from Program Trading; second favourite when 2l fourth to Notable Speech in Grade 1 Breeders' Cup Mile at Del Mar following month; strong chance and goes well fresh.
runners.comment: Five wins from seven starts, include Grade 1 over C&D last October; has good record fresh
runners.owner: Gary Barber Cheyenne Stable LLC & Wachtel Stable

runners.name: Brilliant Berti
runners.spotlight: First Grade 1 attempt when 1l third to Rhetorical in Coolmore Turf Mile here last October, running on well; fourth in Grade 3 at Fairgrounds on reappearance, shaping as if in need of race in steadily run race; outside chance of reaching the frame, if regaining threads of improvement.
runners.comment: Improved in 2025, winning a Grade 2 and close third in Grade 1; needed run on comeback

runners.name: Deterministic
runners.spotlight: Three wins from four starts in 2025, notably Grade 1s at Saratoga in June (1m1f Manhattan Stakes; made all and held on well in blanket finish) and August (1m Fourstardave Stakes; tracked leader, edged just over 1l clear late on); form is a few pounds short of the best here but he could improve again.
runners.comment: Again progressive in 2025, winning pair of Grade 1s at Saratoga last summer; fair claims
runners.owner: St Elias Stable Ken Langone Et Al

runners.name: One Stripe
runners.spotlight: Ex-South African colt where dual Group 1 winner; last in 2025 Breeders' Cup Mile but progressive this winter, winning an allowance at Gulfstream Park (1m, impressive) before neck second to Test Score in Grade 1 Pegasus World Cup Turf (1m1f) there, travelling well and running on strongly; good claims.
runners.comment: Group 1 winner in South Africa beaten a neck in Grade 1 Pegasus World Cup Turf; big chance

runners.name: Troubleshooting
runners.spotlight: Progressive in 2025 and won four times, notably Grade 1 Ainsworth Franklin-Simpson Stakes at Kentucky Downs in October by half a length, forced wide; also won Grade 3 over C&D in November by a neck; has won both starts here, but best form is not on same level as the pick of this tough field.
runners.comment: Progressive in 2025, winning weaker Grade 1 at Kentucky Downs and C&D Grade 3; needs more

runners.name: Tiz Dashing
runners.spotlight: Improved form to gain second Grade 3 win in 8.5f Canadian Turf at Gulfstream Park on reappearance, making smooth headway to challenge and asserting readily to win by nearly 4l; benefited from strong pace on that occasion and likely needs a similar set-up to win this; intriguing nonetheless.
runners.comment: Improved in Grade 3 romp at Gulfstream Park (came from off strong pace); intriguing

runners.name: Tenacious Leader
runners.spotlight: Impressive winner in 2025 of a maiden on dirt (1m, taken off turf) at Saratoga in August and allowance on turf (8.5f) here in October; also runner-up twice at Grade 3 level; favourite, only fifth in turf allowance at Gulfstream Park on reappearance; lots to prove.
runners.comment: Won maiden and an allowance in 2025; also second twice in Grade 3s; lots to prove

runners.name: Pitkin
runners.spotlight: Reserve coming off maiden and allowance wins, both at Gulfstream Park; impressive in 8.5f track-record time on second occasion, but the surface was very fast and he won by less than 1l; an interesting colt when tacking lesser Grades than this.
runners.comment: Reserve; broke 8.5f track record at Gulfstream on very firm; interesting at lower levels

runners.name: Bear River
runners.spotlight: Made all in Grade 2 Kentucky Turf Sprint (6f) last August, holding on well by half a length; mixed form otherwise, including when ninth in Grade 2 over 5.5f here last week; best form over shorter and he would spring a huge surprise if outlasting these.
runners.comment: Grade 2-winning sprinter last August, only ninth in similar event last week; hard to fancy

runners.name: Wolfies Dynaghost
runners.spotlight: 14 career wins in 33 races, latterly 1m1f Grade 3s at Churchill Downs in November and Gulfstream Park (track record) in December; odds-on when only fifth in 8.5f Grade 3 on reappearance; can go a good gallop, but likely in too deep here (only sixth in his one Grade 1).
runners.comment: Pacey and prolific scorer, including in two 2025 Grade 3s; severely tested at this level

runners.name: Zulu Kingdom
runners.spotlight: Talented miler, winner six times in eight and also demoted from first once; justified favouritism in Grade 1 American Turf on 2025 Kentucky Derby card; shaped like best horse, but mildly hampered rival and placed fourth, in Grade 2 at Saratoga in August; absent almost nine months since.
runners.comment: First past post last four starts of 2025, notably Grade 1, but off since demoted in August
runners.owner: Madaket Stables LLC Michael Dubb Et Al
